chapters.description: Students who were seven in 2002 and seventeen in 2012. This cohort experienced ten years of high-stakes testing under NCLB. Despite reaching record highs at age thirteen (2008), they showed a statistically significant decline in reading ability by age seventeen.
key_passages: Students who were seven in 2002 would have been old NCLB hands in 2012. They would have experienced almost ten years of high-stakes testing in reading under NCLB.
This 2012 result for seventeen-year-olds seems particularly anomalous, because that same cohort four years earlier, at age thirteen, had attained the topmost score that NAEP had ever recorded
contestable_aspects: Whether the decline was caused by NCLB or other social/cultural factors.

chapters.description: The period following the passage of the No Child Left Behind Act, characterized by high-stakes testing in math and reading, which led to a significant narrowing of the broader elementary curriculum.
key_passages: It is widely conceded that the high-stakes testing introduced by No Child Left Behind (NCLB) in 2002 caused a reduction in the knowledge students have gained in history, civics, music, visual art, literature, and science.
The current focus on test preparation has therefore led to a hyper-emphasis on general skills. Skills and tests have now become the main subject matters of our elementary schools.

cases.name: New York City Core Knowledge Pilot

chapters.description: A 20 school study (10 experimental, 10 control) of Core Knowledge. Despite 'overwhelmingly superior' results, the incoming chancellor dismissed the study as 'too small' and returned to 'balanced literacy' rather than expanding the knowledge-based approach.
key_passages: It showed overwhelmingly superior results for Core Knowledge. When the new chancellor in New York City arrived on the scene, she dismissed the study as being 'too small,' but did not propose a bigger one.

cases.name: New York City Schools Chancellor Carmen Fariña

chapters.description: Chancellor Fariña dismissed the positive data from Core Knowledge schools in NYC because the results conflicted with her deep-seated 'balanced literacy' and developmentalist beliefs.
key_passages: That explains the scornful dismissal by New York City Schools Chancellor Carmen Fariña of the superior results achieved by the Core Knowledge reading program in New York City. She had a deeper certitude than mere data could supply about what is best for children.
contestable_aspects: The Chancellor's motivations for rejecting the data; the statistical significance of the Core Knowledge results in that specific context.

chapters.description: Federal legislation enacted in 2002 that mandated grade-by-grade reading standards and high-stakes testing. Hirsch notes it improved decoding but led to a decline in knowledge-based instruction.
key_passages: The No Child Left Behind Act, which was signed into law in 2002, mandated standards and yearly high-stakes testing in reading and math based on the state standards.
Younger students can now decode texts with more fluency and accuracy than they did before NCLB.

cases.name: PARCC (Partnership for Assessment of Readiness for College and Careers)

chapters.description: One of the two primary Common Core test consortia. The author uses their Grade 5 practice test as evidence of the 'mirage' of logical inference in close-reading questions, where students are expected to deduce word meanings from context clues that are actually insufficient without prior knowledge.
key_passages: The Partnership for Assessment of Readiness for College and Careers (PARCC), one of the two Common Core test consortia, has created two-part close-reading questions.

chapters.description: The two primary multi-state testing consortia developed to create assessments for the Common Core State Standards. Hirsch uses their sample items to show that the new tests are identical in flaw to the old ones.
key_passages: The sample items made public by the new test consortia for the Common Core amply fulfill my prediction that the tests will stress both main-idea finding and close reading.
Thus PARCC, one of the two test consortia, has developed two-part questions...
The 'Smarter Balanced' consortium puts the two-part structure into a single question...
contestable_aspects: The intent of the test developers regarding 'close reading' vs. 'background knowledge'.

chapters.description: Specific schools cited as examples where the adoption of a knowledge-based curriculum produced observable success and boosted teacher/student morale and efficacy.
key_passages: When people see the evidence of success with their own eyes, as in PS 124 and the Washington School, they gain a sense of efficacy.

chapters.description: A K-8 neighborhood school with a high poverty rate (80% free lunch) and proximity to a homeless shelter. Under leadership from Elain Thompson and Valarie Lewis, the school rejected skill-drill mandates in favor of the Core Knowledge Sequence starting in 1999. The school eventually closed the achievement gap, won national awards, and outperformed the NYC average on Common Core tests.
key_passages: In 1999 they decided to persuade their staff to follow the Core Knowledge Sequence.
In 2014, on New York’s tough new Common Core–aligned tests, we outperformed the New York City average.
Our test results show that we closed the achievement gap under No Child Left Behind.
contestable_aspects: The degree to which the Core Knowledge curriculum was the primary variable versus the leadership of Thompson and Lewis.
The replicability of 'working around' district regulations.

cases.name: PS/MS 124 (The Yung Wing School), Queens, NYC

chapters.description: A high-poverty school that adopted Core Knowledge in 1998. The transformation led to increased teacher retention, soaring student test scores in science and social studies that rivaled wealthy districts, and a culture of academic engagement extending to the playground.
key_passages: As our implementation of Core Knowledge deepened, our fifth graders’ achievement soared.
One of the most important was that the entire school became connected.
The reality of a haphazard skills curriculum adjusted to the individual child is not as happy as its individualistic theory proposes.
contestable_aspects: Whether the success was due specifically to the content or to the leadership and collaborative culture established by the principal.

chapters.description: A hypothetical example of a two-week curriculum unit used to demonstrate how students who start unable to read high-Lexile texts on a topic become capable of reading them after sustained exposure to that specific domain.
key_passages: Under a coherent common curriculum, texts with high Lexile scores might have been unreadable for many students at the beginning of a two-week unit on a domain like “Plants and Chlorophyll,” but will become highly readable by the end of the unit.

chapters.description: One of the most ambitious longitudinal studies in U.S. history, reported in the 1970s and 80s, designed to determine which instructional methods best helped disadvantaged students. It famously found that Direct Instruction was the most effective method, while developmental and Reggio Emilia approaches were the least effective.
key_passages: In the case of Direct Instruction, the chief evidence came from one of the most ambitious longitudinal studies we ever carried out: Project Follow Through.

Developmental, natural-growth, Reggio Emilia–style preschools had the least effect; deliberate, step-by-step skill instruction had the greater positive effect... Leading all the rest on all measures was Direct Instruction.

chapters.description: A teacher who secretly used phonics instruction instead of the mandated balanced literacy; despite her students performing above the norm, she received a negative evaluation because she failed to use constructivist methods.
key_passages: After graduation, she secretly taught her students to read via the phonics method, and they performed above the norm... she received an unfavorable report. She had not followed methods of “whole language” or “balanced literacy”

chapters.description: Twelve-year-old students were divided based on their reading test scores and their baseball knowledge. The study found that students categorized as 'poor readers' but who had high baseball knowledge significantly outperformed 'good readers' who lacked baseball knowledge when both groups read a text about baseball.
key_passages: In 1988, Recht and Leslie showed that 'poor readers' outperformed 'good readers' when the poor readers knew more about the subject matter—in this case, baseball.
When a topic is familiar, 'poor' readers become 'good' readers; moreover, when a topic is unfamiliar, normally better readers lose their advantage.

chapters.description: An individualized, natural-development model of preschool in Italy where 'no wrong answers' exist and formal instruction is avoided. The author argues its success is dependent on its middle-class demographic.
key_passages: the children in Reggio Emilia are nearly all from middle-class homes, so much of the work into the communal standard language has already been fostered by parents.
We don’t believe in formal instruction... We try to nurture each child’s innate desire to communicate and develop their joy of learning.

chapters.description: A local radio show hosted by Andy Brownell and Tom Ostrom that discussed why the successful Core Knowledge curriculum wasn't expanded citywide. The discussion highlighted the myriad of ideological and political objections raised by the community.
key_passages: The topic of the radio program was to be this: Given the unfilled popular demand for the Core Knowledge Sequence, why doesn’t Rochester make that cost-free Sequence the basis for the elementary curriculum in all the schools of the city?
Ostrom had no problem recounting the objections he had heard throughout the city against the idea of adopting such a curriculum sequence citywide

chapters.description: A study that replicated the Recht-Leslie finding but used IQ as a variable instead of reading test scores. It showed that children with low IQs but high knowledge of soccer outperformed high-IQ children who lacked soccer knowledge when reading about the sport.
key_passages: The knowledge variables in their experiments were soccer knowledge versus soccer ignorance, and the variables were not higher and lower reading scores, but higher and lower IQs!
it showed the extent to which topic-relevant knowledge trumps basic cognitive differences.

chapters.description: A movement in St. Louis led by W.T. Harris and Susan Blow that established the city as a center for Hegelian philosophy and founded the nation's first public kindergarten.
key_passages: By the 1860s and 1870s St. Louis equaled Harvard as a center of Hegelian thought.
There was also Susan Blow, another St. Louis Hegelian like Harris, who started the nation’s first public kindergarten in 1873.

chapters.description: The current mode of schooling where teachers are told that subject matter is secondary and that comprehension strategies can be taught using trivial texts like 'Tyler Makes Pancakes!' rather than substantive content like biographies of Lincoln.
key_passages: Teachers have been told that subject matter is secondary, that they can teach strategies just as well with Tyler Makes Pancakes! or Stupendous Sports Stadiums as with a biography of Abraham Lincoln.

chapters.description: The evaluation of the Success for All model as part of President Obama's Investing in Innovation (i3) program. The 2015 results showed that when the program was scaled up to a broad range of real-world schools, it failed to produce better reading outcomes than control groups, demonstrating the persistence of fadeout even in 'successful' skills-based programs.
key_passages: Success for All, after many years of proving its high effectiveness, had given us every reason to hope... Not so. When scaled up so that credible real-world evaluations can now be made, it has been found that Success for All does not avoid fadeout.

chapters.description: Following a 1994 law, Sweden moved from a common national curriculum to an individualized model focused on 'thinking skills.' This led to a significant decline in PISA scores and a massive deterioration in social equality, making it a parallel to the French experience.
key_passages: In the primary grades, the coherence of the common curriculum disappeared.
Now in the PISA studies Sweden is second only to France as the country that has deteriorated the most in social equality.

chapters.description: Sweden transitioned from a highly centralized, egalitarian system with a national curriculum to a decentralized system emphasizing school choice, market competition, and individualized learning. The reform allowed local schools and teachers to determine content. The result was a dramatic drop in PISA scores and a significant increase in the achievement gap between social classes.
key_passages: In 2014, after a decade of the experiment, the Swedish government commissioned a report from Professor Leif Lewin regarding the effects of its decentralization of Swedish schools

Previously, efforts to create curricula and syllabi had been regarded as a technical matter for administrators and subject-matter experts... In contrast, the basic idea of 1994 law was that henceforth teachers and principals are considered to be in the best position to design the curriculum
Between 2000 and 2012, Swedish PISA verbal scores fell from 516... to 483... It is the largest drop ever recorded by PISA.
contestable_aspects: The degree to which free-market vouchers vs. curriculum changes caused the decline.
The influence of changing demographics during the same period.

cases.name: Switzerland as the Cradle of Progressive Education

chapters.description: The author identifies Switzerland as the birthplace of progressive educational thought through the work of Rousseau, Pestalozzi, and Albertine Necker de Saussure. This tradition focused on child-centered approaches and the 'natural stages' of development.
key_passages: In emphasizing Hegel and Froebel and Germany I do not mean to overlook Switzerland, a cradle of progressive education with Rousseau ... and Pestalozzi.
From Switzerland also came the first significant use of the term progressive education, the title of an 1828 book by Swiss writer Albertine Necker de Saussure.

chapters.description: Hirsch compares the specific, building-block nature of Texas math standards (fractions) with the vague, repetitive reading standards that focus solely on 'main ideas' from grade 3 through grade 8.
key_passages: Current math standards are much better guides for teachers and test makers than are current standards in reading.
Wary of specifying either topics or texts, the standards makers focus on the skill of finding the main idea.

chapters.description: The author uses released test questions from Texas to demonstrate how test items are framed to look like they are testing strategies (like inferencing or summarizing) while they are actually testing the student's ability to understand the specific content of the passage.
key_passages: Here are some 'stems' taken from released items on the Texas tests. Their form indicates misleadingly that strategy expertise rather than specific knowledge is being probed.
To keep Texas still in view, these stems were taken from released test questions in Texas for fifth and seventh graders.

chapters.description: The author examines released items from Texas and Virginia because they did not initially adopt Common Core, providing examples of 'traditional' strategy-based testing. These tests use question stems that pretend to measure 'inferencing skills' but actually rely on passage familiarity.
key_passages: I offer examples from Texas and Virginia, since those states did not sign on to the Common Core, and the standards are in force.
Their form indicates misleadingly that strategy expertise rather than specific knowledge is being probed.

chapters.description: Hirsch reflects on the list in the back of his original book, noting it was frequently 'pirated' and misinterpreted as an elitist inventory rather than a descriptive map of the knowledge required for middle-class economic participation.
key_passages: The list at the back of the book was actually a list of what literate Americans who made a good living and could communicate with other Americans in fact did know in 1987
the book itself was not much read compared to the list at the back, which circulated widely in pirated packets

chapters.description: A deliberate effort in the US to expand the canon and shared vocabulary to include women and non-Anglo minorities, which resulted in additions to the 'cultural literacy' list like the term 'Anglo.'
key_passages: The slow mixing process in America became both conscious and political in the 1980s and 1990s, as part of a leveling campaign to diminish male WASP dominance, and to elevate the status of women and nonwhite, non-Anglo minorities.

chapters.description: A period where Core Knowledge was criticized as elitist for being traditional, while intellectuals sought to valorize neglected cultures and deprivilege the dominant culture.
key_passages: Core Knowledge plan was necessarily traditional, and was criticized in the 1990s for being so. It appeared to perpetuate the dominance of the already dominant elements of American life.

chapters.description: A test of whether a nation conceived in liberty and equality can endure, which the author argues depends on a shared 'political religion' taught in schools.
key_passages: The test of democratic government—determining whether it perishes from the earth—was to be whether it was sustained through ‘reverence for the laws’ and the founding proposition of equality

chapters.description: A historical period and educational goal where language arts teachers used classic, common texts to socialize and unify the American population through a shared cultural stock.
key_passages: Language arts teachers were formerly key figures in the Americanization project.
In an age when the need of socializing and unifying our people is keenly felt, the value of a common stock of knowledge, a common set of ideals is obvious.
contestable_aspects: The degree to which 'Americanization' was inclusive versus exclusionary or assimilationist.

chapters.description: Marietta Johnson's school for the well-to-do, which implemented 'natural development' ideas without a perceived need for communal knowledge initiation.
key_passages: Mrs. Johnson’s famous school in Fairhope, Alabama, was a school for the well-to-do, for whom basic initiation into communal knowledge was scarcely needed.

chapters.description: The storming of the Bastille during the French Revolution served as a catalyst for an 'orgy of historical optimism' and the widespread adoption of pantheistic feelings in Europe, directly influencing thinkers like Hegel and Wordsworth who would later influence American education.
key_passages: In Europe, three decades earlier, in 1789, a Paris mob had stormed the Bastille prison... starting the French Revolution. Coming after the American Revolution of 1776, the two revolutions taken together induced an orgy of historical optimism.
The God-as-nature idea... did not become a widespread sentiment until after the fall of the Bastille.
contestable_aspects: The direct causal link between the Bastille and the widespread adoption of pantheism among educators.

chapters.description: France adopted American-style child-centered, individualistic educational theories and subsequently suffered a massive decline in student achievement, paralleling the American experience.
key_passages: When the French later adopted those same ideas they suffered a decline of similar massive proportions. Our “Nation at Risk” of 1983 became their “Crise de l’école” of 2007.
The French have now repeated our experiment in educational individualism in a more concentrated and better-documented form.
contestable_aspects: The causal link between specific ideological shifts and the French test score decline.

chapters.description: France adopted American-style individualistic and naturalistic educational ideas, leading to a decline in student outcomes that mirrored the earlier American decline.
key_passages: After 1989, the French in effect duplicated the American decline of the 1960s and 1970s by means of the same basic change in guiding ideas.

cases.name: The Jelly Beans vs. Tree Frogs Comparison

chapters.description: An example cited to illustrate that third graders can handle high text complexity (sentences and rare words) when the topic is familiar (jelly beans) but fail on even simpler texts when the topic is unfamiliar (tree frogs).
key_passages: When a third grader confronts a text about tree frogs... most don’t know about tree frogs. They get stymied. But when they are given a technically harder text about jelly beans, they read with ease.

chapters.description: A growing protest by parents and teachers against excessive testing and test preparation. It is characterized by significant numbers of districts refusing tests, teachers complaining about the narrowing of the curriculum, and parents reporting extreme stress and health issues in their children.
key_passages: The opt-out protest against excessive testing and test preparation has spread to significant numbers of districts.
Parents railed at a system that they said was overrun by new tests coming from all levels—district, state and federal.

cases.name: The Plowden Era in Great Britain

chapters.description: Following the 1967 Plowden Report, British primary education shifted toward an informal, child-centered style emphasizing individualization and discovery, which critics blamed for a decline in educational standards and increased inequality.
key_passages: The Plowden Report encouraged schools to 'develop a more informal, child-centered style of education with an emphasis on individualization and learning by discovery'.

chapters.description: The pedagogical reforms implemented by Francis Parker in Quincy, Massachusetts, which emphasized active, visual, and tactile learning while initially maintaining a subject-matter curriculum.
key_passages: Then he took over as the school superintendent of Quincy, Massachusetts. While Parker wanted a strong, common subject-matter curriculum in the early grades, his emphasis was on pedagogy, on the active, visual, tactile (natural) experience of such a curriculum.

chapters.description: In contemporary American schools, particularly in language arts, a 'benign' focus on the individual child combined with the 'mirage' of general skills has resulted in a curriculum dominated by repetitive drills. These drills are intended to build general thinking skills but result in neither actual skill proficiency nor success on standardized tests.
key_passages: It has induced an ever-more-desperate effort to gain nonexistent skills through soul-deadening drills.

A benign child-centeredness coupled with a faulty theory about general skills has led us to a child purgatory of skill drills. These have produced neither good skills nor good scores on the ever-looming tests.
contestable_aspects: The characterization of skill instruction as 'soul-deadening drills' vs. necessary practice.
The causal link between child-centeredness and the rise of drills.

chapters.description: An early Core Knowledge school where teachers were initially intimidated by the content they had to teach but eventually saw success in morale and test scores.
key_passages: They were intimidated that first year by having to teach things they did not know themselves. The next year when I visited, they were enthusiastically explaining that they were learning these things along with the children. They started having a signal success in improved morale of students and teachers, and improved test outcomes.

chapters.description: Nations like Finland, Canada, Japan, and Korea demonstrate that high achievement and narrowed economic gaps occur when school curriculum is explicit and known to all.
key_passages: Those nations whose elementary schools perform the best overall—Finland, Canada, Japan, Korea—also rank high in narrowing the achievement gaps between economic groups. In the highest-scoring nations the year-to-year curriculum topics are known to all—students, teachers, and parents.

chapters.description: A period labeled by Hirsch as the 'silver age' where US schools performed high internationally in both excellence and equity before the decline.
key_passages: As late as the 1940s and 1950s the public education of the United States, for all its racial and social shortcomings, scored near the top among nations in both achievement and equality.
So let’s call the higher-scoring era before the decline—the 1940s and 1950s—a “silver age.”
contestable_aspects: Whether this era was actually superior in terms of equity given segregation.

chapters.description: The transition in American literacy materials from the knowledge-rich, moral, and patriotic focus of Webster and McGuffey to a focus on 'cultivating imagination,' marked by the victory of fairy tale proponents.
key_passages: In earlier times, the readings in Noah Webster’s Blue Back Speller and W. H. McGuffey’s Eclectic Readers were directed to making moral and patriotic citizens.
The fairy tale proponents won out in due course, because by the 1850s “cultivation of the imagination” had started to become an accepted educational goal.

chapters.description: The author characterizes high student mobility (30%+) within urban districts as a primary cause of educational failure for poor students, which can only be solved by district-wide curricular uniformity.
key_passages: the movement of disadvantaged students from school to school in the middle of the year for economic reasons. This movement of poor students occurs mostly within the district—an inconvenient truth. In most cities, the mobility rate is around 30 percent...

chapters.description: Major jurisdictions including Chicago, Washington DC, and New York City have implemented value-added measures for teacher evaluation and sanctions.
key_passages: Some jurisdictions, including Chicago, Washington, DC, and New York City, have instituted value-added measures (VAMs) of teacher effectiveness

chapters.description: The author cites the Virginia Standards of Learning as a prime example of 'content emptiness,' where from grade 2 through grade 8, the curriculum requirement for reading is simply the repetitive instruction to 'Identify the main idea.'
key_passages: My own state of Virginia is more forthright about the inherent repetitiousness and content emptiness of its reading standards: Grade 2: 'g) Identify the main idea.' Grade 3: 'g) Identify the main idea.' ... Grade 8: 'h) Identify the main idea.'

canonical_claim.text: Current standardized reading tests are based on a wrong theory of inference and reading comprehension.

members.text: Reading tests are actually knowledge tests in disguise.
Standardized reading tests are actually probes of a student's knowledge and vocabulary.
Standardized reading comprehension tests are primarily measures of communal knowledge and vocabulary.
Current standardized reading tests are based on a wrong theory of inference and reading comprehension.

canonical_claim.text: The decline of substantive content in early elementary grades was caused by naturalism and individualism taking over American classrooms in the 1920s and 1930s.

members.text: The French educational decline after 1989 was a duplication of the American decline of the 1960s and 1970s, caused by a change in guiding ideas.
The decline in American public schooling quality began in the 1960s and 1970s.
The primary cause of changes in French educational outcomes after 1989 was the change in curriculum and pedagogy.
The decline of substantive content in early elementary grades was caused by naturalism and individualism taking over American classrooms in the 1920s and 1930s.

canonical_claim.text: A systematic approach to content that includes all students in the class is the most effective method for students to gain new knowledge, interests, and skills.

members.text: Domain immersion is the most effective strategy for enhancing knowledge and vocabulary in preschool.
A systematic approach to content that includes all students in the class is the most effective method for students to gain new knowledge, interests, and skills.
Narratives are the most effective psychological format for conveying knowledge to students.

canonical_claim.text: Schools have a duty in a modern democracy to transmit the shared knowledge and cultural commons of the nation's public sphere.

members.text: Shared knowledge in the elementary curriculum is the primary 'nourisher' of the public sphere.
Schools have a duty in a modern democracy to transmit the shared knowledge and cultural commons of the nation's public sphere.
Every child in a democracy deserves access to the shared, enabling knowledge and language of the de facto public commons.

canonical_claim.text: Imparting a well-rounded, knowledge-based curriculum is the solution to preschool fadeout and the achievement gap.

members.text: Knowledge-based schooling can significantly ameliorate the achievement gap.
Imparting a well-rounded, knowledge-based curriculum is the solution to preschool fadeout and the achievement gap.
A well-defined, cumulative curriculum is the primary key to closing the achievement gap.

canonical_claim.text: The doctrine of 'general skills' (like critical thinking) is used as a defense for fragmented and individualized curricula.

members.text: The unifying aim of education is to develop critical thinking and other general skills.
The doctrine of 'general skills' (like critical thinking) is used as a defense for fragmented and individualized curricula.
The theory of all-purpose skills is used as a justification for a fragmented and haphazard curriculum.

canonical_claim.text: Vocabulary size is determined by the cumulative knowledge acquired from birth to age seventeen, not by high school instruction alone.

members.text: Vocabulary size is the result of slow, multiyear accretion.
A seventeen-year-old's vocabulary size is a cumulative result of knowledge acquired gradually from birth.
Vocabulary size is determined by the cumulative knowledge acquired from birth to age seventeen, not by high school instruction alone.

canonical_claim.text: A student's ability to identify a main idea on a test is a direct result of their understanding of the specific passage, not a transferable skill.

members.text: The idea that there is a generalized, transferable 'main-idea-finding skill' is a myth.
A student's ability to identify a main idea on a test is a direct result of their understanding of the specific passage, not a transferable skill.
The ability to find the main idea of a text depends entirely on the student's prior knowledge and vocabulary.

canonical_claim.text: There is a scientific consensus among cognitive scientists that relevant knowledge is the primary driver of reading comprehension once decoding is mastered.

members.text: There is a scientific consensus among cognitive scientists that relevant knowledge is the primary driver of reading comprehension once decoding is mastered.
After decoding is mastered, relevant knowledge is the skill set that most reliably determines reading comprehension.
Systematic knowledge building is the primary driver of high reading ability.

canonical_claim.text: The common educational view that curriculum content is merely a vehicle for gaining general reading skills is a primary cause of fadeout.

members.text: The common educational view that curriculum content is merely a vehicle for gaining general reading skills is a primary cause of fadeout.
The disintegration of the primary school curriculum is the primary cause of the French educational crisis.
The elementary curriculum is the primary cause of the fadeout problem at all ages.

canonical_claim.text: The transmission of shared, traditional knowledge is essential to human development and the realization of equal opportunity in a democracy.

members.text: Initiation into shared knowledge of the standard language is a prerequisite for realizing the ideal of equal opportunity in a democracy.
Broadly shared communal knowledge is a cognitive prerequisite for the effective use of language.
The transmission of shared, traditional knowledge is essential to human development and the realization of equal opportunity in a democracy.

canonical_claim.text: There is a causal connection between the child-friendly individualism of modern education and the failure to narrow verbal achievement gaps.

members.text: There is a causal connection between the child-friendly individualism of modern education and the failure to narrow verbal achievement gaps.
Providential individualism is a primary cause of the failure to narrow achievement gaps in the United States, Britain, France, and Sweden.
The educational philosophy of child-friendly individualism has contributed to the stall in closing verbal achievement gaps.

canonical_claim.text: The massive decline in American verbal test scores between 1960 and 1980 was caused by curriculum reforms rooted in progressive ideas from the 1930s and 1940s.

members.text: American test scores declined nationwide between 1960 and 1980.
The massive decline in American verbal test scores between 1960 and 1980 was caused by curriculum reforms rooted in progressive ideas from the 1930s and 1940s.

canonical_claim.text: Novices are hindered in information seeking because the human mind can only assimilate three or four new items before working memory is exhausted.

members.text: Human working memory is limited.
Novices are hindered in information seeking because the human mind can only assimilate three or four new items before working memory is exhausted.

canonical_claim.text: Six major educational frustrations in the United States—over-testing, preschool fade-out, narrowed curriculum, low high school verbal scores, stagnant achievement gaps, and Common Core struggles—are caused by the reign of faulty ideas.

members.text: Ideas are reflected in architecture.

Six major educational frustrations in the United States—over-testing, preschool fade-out, narrowed curriculum, low high school verbal scores, stagnant achievement gaps, and Common Core struggles—are caused by the reign of faulty ideas.

canonical_claim.text: The narrowing of reading achievement gaps in early grades is due to improved fluency scores, not long-term literacy gains.

members.text: Listening ability in early grades is the primary key to later reading ability.
The narrowing of reading achievement gaps in early grades is due to improved fluency scores, not long-term literacy gains.

canonical_claim.text: The Swedish reform of 1994 substituted specific subject content with a focus on generic 'thinking skills' such as critical thinking and problem solving.

members.text: Thinking skills such as 'critical thinking' and 'problem solving' are not productive educational aims.
The Swedish reform of 1994 substituted specific subject content with a focus on generic 'thinking skills' such as critical thinking and problem solving.

canonical_claim.text: Treating reading as a general, content-independent skill is a failed educational paradigm that yields little long-term benefit.

members.text: There is no such thing as developing a general, content-independent skill.
Treating reading as a general, content-independent skill is a failed educational paradigm that yields little long-term benefit.

canonical_claim.text: The achievement gap between social groups is primarily a knowledge gap and a language gap rather than a result of genetics or sociology.

members.text: The achievement gap between social groups is primarily a knowledge gap and a language gap rather than a result of genetics or sociology.
The knowledge differential between social classes is a major cause of the income differential.

canonical_claim.text: Early education should be appropriate to the child's age and nature as part of a natural developmental process.

members.text: Early education should be appropriate to the child's age and nature as part of a natural developmental process.
The content of early education should not be left to the 'nature of the child' or the 'market.'

canonical_claim.text: Instruction based on learning styles and multiple intelligences is not truly child-centered and perpetuates social inequality.

members.text: Current educational assumptions lead to instruction that is not actually child-centered and perpetuates social inequality.
Instruction based on learning styles and multiple intelligences is not truly child-centered and perpetuates social inequality.

canonical_claim.text: From 1975 to 1985, France possessed the highest achieving and most egalitarian school system of any large country in Europe.

members.text: From 1975 to 1985, France possessed the highest achieving and most egalitarian school system of any large country in Europe.
In the 1980s, France and Sweden possessed the best large school systems in Europe in terms of achievement and equity.

canonical_claim.text: Critical thinking does not exist as a domain-general construct distinct from general cognitive ability.

members.text: A single, overarching general skill does not exist.
Critical thinking does not exist as a domain-general construct distinct from general cognitive ability.

canonical_claim.text: The primary objective of this book is to promote the general communal principle of education rather than to advocate for any specific curriculum product like the Core Knowledge Sequence.

members.text: The primary objective of this book is to promote the general communal principle of education rather than to advocate for any specific curriculum product like the Core Knowledge Sequence.
The success of Core Knowledge schools validates the principle of a specific, communal, grade-by-grade curriculum rather than just a specific program.

canonical_claim.text: General skills such as close reading, complexity managing, and main-idea finding do not actually exist as transferable skills.

members.text: General skills such as close reading, complexity managing, and main-idea finding do not actually exist as transferable skills.
There is no general main-idea-finding skill, complexity-managing skill, or close-reading skill.

canonical_claim.text: The chief aim of preschool from the standpoint of social justice is to enhance children's knowledge, syntax, and vocabularies.

members.text: The primary objective of preschool from an equal-opportunity perspective is to enhance children's knowledge and vocabularies.
The chief aim of preschool from the standpoint of social justice is to enhance children's knowledge, syntax, and vocabularies.

canonical_claim.text: Vocabulary catch-up occurs by implicit word learning when domain immersion is applied consistently with a sequence of domains and independent readings.

members.text: Vocabulary catch-up is achieved through a natural process of implicit word learning when domain immersion is sequenced across multiple years.
Vocabulary catch-up occurs by implicit word learning when domain immersion is applied consistently with a sequence of domains and independent readings.

canonical_claim.text: The educational tradition of 'natural development' is factually incorrect and has caused the anti-intellectualism of modern American education.

members.text: The educational tradition of 'natural development' is factually incorrect and has caused the anti-intellectualism of modern American education.
Natural development theories are factually incorrect.

canonical_claim.text: National NAEP reading comprehension scores for seventeen-year-olds serve as critical indices of the nation's achievement of twenty-first-century skills.

members.text: National NAEP reading comprehension scores for seventeen-year-olds serve as critical indices of the nation's achievement of twenty-first-century skills.
Reading comprehension scores of seventeen-year-olds serve as a proxy for their future life chances.

canonical_claim.text: The Japanese science curriculum is a model of an intentionally and coherently structured curriculum designed to build rich content knowledge across grades.

members.text: Students gain a foundation of knowledge only when the curriculum is intentionally and coherently structured across grades.
The Japanese science curriculum is a model of an intentionally and coherently structured curriculum designed to build rich content knowledge across grades.

canonical_claim.text: The modern focus on 'imagination' in education represents a shift from Noah Webster's classical instructive model to a Romantic 'inner light' model.

members.text: The educational focus on developing imagination represents a shift from communal education to the Romantic idea of natural individualism.
The modern focus on 'imagination' in education represents a shift from Noah Webster's classical instructive model to a Romantic 'inner light' model.

canonical_claim.text: Teaching children substantial knowledge about the world is more valuable to their well-being, creativity, and imagination than emphasizing fanciful stories intended to encourage imagination.

members.text: Teaching children substantial knowledge about the world is more valuable to their well-being, creativity, and imagination than emphasizing fanciful stories intended to encourage imagination.
Teaching children substantial knowledge of the world is more valuable to their future well-being than teaching 'imaginative' stories.

canonical_claim.text: The educational concept of child development as a 'natural unfolding' has interfered with the primary goal of early education, which is socialization.

members.text: The educational concept of child development as a 'natural unfolding' has interfered with the primary goal of early education, which is socialization.
Socialization is more natural to the human child than 'natural unfolding' is.

canonical_claim.text: The American educational decline was caused by the adoption of an early-nineteenth-century Middle European educational ideology (progressive education).

members.text: Germany served as the nineteenth-century nursery of progressive education.
The American educational decline was caused by the adoption of an early-nineteenth-century Middle European educational ideology (progressive education).

canonical_claim.text: Antonio Gramsci argued that the common school should be a 'school of humanistic formation' or a 'school of general culture.'

members.text: Antonio Gramsci advocated for a 'unitary school' of general culture to provide humanistic formation for all children.
Antonio Gramsci argued that the common school should be a 'school of humanistic formation' or a 'school of general culture.'

canonical_claim.text: Developmental psychology has shown that 'natural development' is an unreliable guide for determining appropriate school content.

members.text: Word frequency is an unreliable guide for determining school curriculum.
Developmental psychology has shown that 'natural development' is an unreliable guide for determining appropriate school content.

canonical_claim.text: Reading test questions about main ideas and inference-making are actually probing knowledge and vocabulary, not general strategies.

members.text: A reading test is effectively a test of general knowledge and vocabulary.
Reading test questions about main ideas and inference-making are actually probing knowledge and vocabulary, not general strategies.

canonical_claim.text: The unpredictability of test topics is an inherent and necessary feature of all-purpose reading tests when the school curriculum is not standardized.

members.text: Reading comprehension is not an all-purpose skill like decoding.
The unpredictability of test topics is an inherent and necessary feature of all-purpose reading tests when the school curriculum is not standardized.

canonical_claim.text: True individuality is achieved only through full membership in the 'tribe' via shared knowledge and conventions.

members.text: Only full membership in the social 'tribe' leads to the development of individuality.
True individuality is achieved only through full membership in the 'tribe' via shared knowledge and conventions.

canonical_claim.text: Reading standards in states like Texas and Virginia are repetitive and 'content empty,' failing to provide a clear progression of topics across grades.

members.text: The reading standards in Virginia are repetitious and lack substantive content across grade levels.
Reading standards in states like Texas and Virginia are repetitive and 'content empty,' failing to provide a clear progression of topics across grades.

canonical_claim.text: Any educational policy that fails to improve reading test scores is a failed policy because valid reading tests measure a student's initiation into the public sphere.

members.text: Initiation into the public sphere is a fundamental aim of education.
Any educational policy that fails to improve reading test scores is a failed policy because valid reading tests measure a student's initiation into the public sphere.

canonical_claim.text: Most of the information needed to understand a text is not provided by the text itself but must be drawn from the reader's prior knowledge.

members.text: The meaning of a text is constructed by combining presented information with the reader's existing knowledge to form a situation model.
Most of the information needed to understand a text is not provided by the text itself but must be drawn from the reader's prior knowledge.

canonical_claim.text: Reading proficiency requires mastering a specific canon of knowledge and vocabulary shared by competent readers.

members.text: Reading proficiency requires mastering a specific canon of knowledge and vocabulary shared by competent readers.
Reading proficiency does not require mastering a specific literary canon.

canonical_claim.text: Progressive, 'modern' educational methods like 'natural' reading and self-learning were already dominant in French teacher-training schools by 1977.

members.text: French education schools were dominated by progressive methods as early as 1977.
Progressive, 'modern' educational methods like 'natural' reading and self-learning were already dominant in French teacher-training schools by 1977.

canonical_claim.text: In 1989, France passed the loi Jospin, which required elementary schools to replace the national curriculum with locally determined, individualized curricula.

In 1989, France passed the loi Jospin, which required elementary schools to replace the national curriculum with locally determined, individualized curricula.

canonical_claim.text: The French longitudinal studies (based on 4,200 students from a cohort of 1 million) yield statistical confidence levels similar to the American NAEP (based on 8,700 students from 6 million).

members.text: French longitudinal studies provide a level of statistical confidence comparable to the American NAEP.
The French longitudinal studies (based on 4,200 students from a cohort of 1 million) yield statistical confidence levels similar to the American NAEP (based on 8,700 students from 6 million).

canonical_claim.text: If a piece of knowledge is not inherently harmful for an advantaged seven-year-old to know, it cannot be logically classified as 'developmentally inappropriate' for a disadvantaged child of the same age.

members.text: If a piece of knowledge is not inherently harmful for an advantaged child to know, it cannot be developmentally inappropriate for a disadvantaged child.

If a piece of knowledge is not inherently harmful for an advantaged seven-year-old to know, it cannot be logically classified as 'developmentally inappropriate' for a disadvantaged child of the same age.

canonical_claim.text: The core of educational 'naturalism' and 'individualism' is the theological assumption that a benevolent purpose is inherent in Nature and will guarantee optimal outcomes if left to take its course.

members.text: Naturalism and individualism in education stem from a theological belief that nature is a manifestation of a benevolent God and is therefore unerring.
The core of educational 'naturalism' and 'individualism' is the theological assumption that a benevolent purpose is inherent in Nature and will guarantee optimal outcomes if left to take its course.

canonical_claim.text: The term 'expertise' is a more accurate description of educational goals than 'skill' because it implies the necessity of knowledge.

The term 'expertise' is a more accurate description of educational goals than 'skill' because it implies the necessity of knowledge.

canonical_claim.text: The focus on teacher quality as the primary lever for reform is a symptom of 'reform fatigue' and desperation after previous structural strategies failed.

The focus on teacher quality as the primary lever for reform is a symptom of 'reform fatigue' and desperation after previous structural strategies failed.

canonical_claim.text: When classroom environments become a 'purgatory' due to punitive reforms, teachers and unions logically shift their focus toward contract stipulations and bread-and-butter concessions.

When classroom environments become a 'purgatory' due to punitive reforms, teachers and unions logically shift their focus toward contract stipulations and bread-and-butter concessions.

canonical_claim.text: Computer programs are inherently limited because they cannot provide the empathetic personal connection that young students rely on for primary education.

Computer programs are inherently limited because they cannot provide the empathetic personal connection that young students rely on for primary education.

canonical_claim.text: The common assertion that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable is a non-footnoted claim that is contradicted by research from Dr. Russ Whitehurst.

The common assertion that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable is a non-footnoted claim that is contradicted by research from Dr. Russ Whitehurst.

canonical_claim.text: Teachers should actively 'rebel' against the skills delusion and insist on a multiyear content-based curriculum for their own professional well-being.

Teachers should actively 'rebel' against the skills delusion and insist on a multiyear content-based curriculum for their own professional well-being.

canonical_claim.text: Schoolteachers should not receive tenure protection in cases where they fail to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.

members.text: Schoolteachers should not receive tenure protection in cases where they fail to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.

canonical_claim.text: Scripted programs like Direct Instruction and Success for All succeeded better than naturalistic approaches in teaching children to sound out printed text.

members.text: Scripted programs like Direct Instruction and Success for All succeeded better than naturalistic approaches in teaching children to sound out printed text.

members.text: The vocabulary gap among poor children in early years is small enough (hundreds of words) to be bridged by effective preschool instruction.
The vocabulary gap between poor and advantaged children in the earliest years is numbered in the hundreds of words, rather than thousands.

canonical_claim.text: The post-1940s acceptance of anti-verbalism and anti-intellectualism in American education is based on the unscientific assumption that tactile and visual learning are more 'natural' than abstract and verbal learning.

canonical_claim.text: Child-centered education carries an implicit danger of genetic determinism regarding both ability and interest.

canonical_claim.text: Reformers and educators should unite around a knowledge-based curriculum by recognizing that verbal skills are foundational to all twenty-first-century skills, including teamwork and communication.

canonical_claim.text: Experts learn faster and more effectively from reference tools like the Internet because their existing knowledge allows them to pay attention only to one or two novel features that integrate easily into memory.

canonical_claim.text: Interviews with parents show that they overwhelmingly prefer a nearby school if it is a good school.

members.text: Interviews with parents show that they overwhelmingly prefer a nearby school if it is a good school.

canonical_claim.text: The true principle of choice includes the 70 percent of parents who wish to stay in their neighborhood and deserve good schools for their children.

members.text: Only approximately 30 percent of parents in inferior schools desire to transport their children to schools outside their neighborhood.
The true principle of choice includes the 70 percent of parents who wish to stay in their neighborhood and deserve good schools for their children.

canonical_claim.text: Schools account for 40% of the differences in student achievement when family background is statistically controlled.

members.text: Schools account for 40% of the differences in student achievement when family background is statistically controlled.
Catholic schools effectively reduced the impact of family background on student achievement.

canonical_claim.text: The interpretation of the Coleman Report that 'family and poverty matter more than schools' provided an excuse for schools to avoid accountability.

members.text: The interpretation of the Coleman Report that 'family and poverty matter more than schools' provided an excuse for schools to avoid accountability.
The popular interpretation of the Coleman Report that family background matters more than schools contains a fundamental logical flaw.

canonical_claim.text: Implicit word learning is more accurate than explicit word study because it correctly builds word connotations from multiple past contexts.

members.text: Implicit word learning is much faster and more accurate than explicit word learning.
Implicit word learning is more accurate than explicit word study because it correctly builds word connotations from multiple past contexts.

canonical_claim.text: Explicit word study should be used sparingly by schools because it can be distortive and consumes excessive time.

members.text: Explicit word study, while useful at times, can be distortive and time-consuming and should be used sparingly.
Explicit word study should be used sparingly by schools because it can be distortive and consumes excessive time.

canonical_claim.text: In language use, a domain represents the relevant prior knowledge that contextualizes discourse and allows for the formation of a situation model.

In language use, a domain represents the relevant prior knowledge that contextualizes discourse and allows for the formation of a situation model.

canonical_claim.text: Great issues of equity and equality of opportunity depend on the application of technical psychological principles in the classroom.

members.text: The achievement of equity and equality of opportunity depends on technical psychological principles of curriculum design.
Great issues of equity and equality of opportunity depend on the application of technical psychological principles in the classroom.

canonical_claim.text: Reading tests must use passages on domains not studied in school because the school curriculum is unstated and unknown to test makers.

members.text: Reading tests must use passages on domains not studied in school because the school curriculum is unstated and unknown to test makers.
Test makers are morally blameless for providing texts on unknown domains because the school curriculum is unstated.

canonical_claim.text: State-of-the-art translation programs like Google Translate fail at understanding because the relationship between close reading of words and actual meaning is inherently indeterminate.

members.text: The relationship between close reading and meaning is inherently indeterminate.
State-of-the-art translation programs like Google Translate fail at understanding because the relationship between close reading of words and actual meaning is inherently indeterminate.

canonical_claim.text: The United States was historically conceived as an 'internal empire' that should tolerate local attachments while spreading systematic ideas.

The United States was historically conceived as an 'internal empire' that should tolerate local attachments while spreading systematic ideas.

canonical_claim.text: The 1989 Jospin law was structurally based on the Bourdieu-Gros Report, which critiqued the national curriculum as an exclusion of non-elite cultures.

members.text: The 1989 Jospin law was structurally based on the Bourdieu-Gros Report, which critiqued the national curriculum as an exclusion of non-elite cultures.
The Bourdieu-Gros Report of 1989 was strikingly vague regarding specific curriculum content.

canonical_claim.text: Bourdieu's 'The Inheritors' is flawed because it draws conclusions about social reproduction based on a single generation of data rather than observing shifts over multiple generations.

members.text: Bourdieu's analysis of social reproduction in 'The Inheritors' is superficial because it only accounts for a single generation's time slice.
Bourdieu's 'The Inheritors' is flawed because it draws conclusions about social reproduction based on a single generation of data rather than observing shifts over multiple generations.

canonical_claim.text: The educational reform in Sweden was based on the theory that individual schools and students, left without state interference, would achieve better results than through state-imposed commonality.

The educational reform in Sweden was based on the theory that individual schools and students, left without state interference, would achieve better results than through state-imposed commonality.

canonical_claim.text: If equality of opportunity is to be achieved, both the political left and right must abandon the goal of individualized curricula in the early grades.

members.text: An effective individualized curriculum in the early grades is an impossible dream if equality of opportunity is the goal.
If equality of opportunity is to be achieved, both the political left and right must abandon the goal of individualized curricula in the early grades.

canonical_claim.text: At present, Core Knowledge is the only published outline of a complete, enabling elementary curriculum for American schools.

members.text: The Core Knowledge Sequence is an example of an enabling elementary curriculum, not the only possible communal curriculum.
At present, Core Knowledge is the only published outline of a complete, enabling elementary curriculum for American schools.

canonical_claim.text: A content-based curriculum is 'necessarily traditional' due to the inescapable inertia of knowledge shared by hundreds of millions of people.

members.text: Knowledge shared among millions of people possesses an inherent and inescapable inertia.
A content-based curriculum is 'necessarily traditional' due to the inescapable inertia of knowledge shared by hundreds of millions of people.

canonical_claim.text: The inclusion of specific cultural figures like Rosa Parks in the curriculum is a result of deliberate activism by cultural activists.

members.text: The inclusion of figures like Rosa Parks in canonical knowledge is the result of deliberate cultural activism.
The inclusion of specific cultural figures like Rosa Parks in the curriculum is a result of deliberate activism by cultural activists.

canonical_claim.text: Vocabulary size is a visible proxy for a student's internal acquisition of knowledge.

members.text: Vocabulary size is a visible proxy for a student's internal acquisition of knowledge.
Vocabulary size is an outward and visible sign of an inward acquisition of knowledge.

canonical_claim.text: Incidental or unconscious word acquisition through topic familiarity is the most efficient and secure way to learn new vocabulary.

members.text: Topic familiarity is the essential 'secret' to both reading comprehension and efficient word learning.
Incidental or unconscious word acquisition through topic familiarity is the most efficient and secure way to learn new vocabulary.

canonical_claim.text: A knowledge-rich curriculum awakens a student's innate desire to learn and fosters spontaneous academic engagement.

members.text: A content-rich curriculum increases student engagement and teacher collaboration.
A knowledge-rich curriculum awakens a student's innate desire to learn and fosters spontaneous academic engagement.

canonical_claim.text: Core Knowledge is not merely a specific program but a curricular principle supported by an immense body of international evidence and research.

members.text: Core Knowledge is not a scripted program, and its specific contents are adaptable by schools.
Core Knowledge is not merely a specific program but a curricular principle supported by an immense body of international evidence and research.

canonical_claim.text: District superintendents could facilitate curriculum reform by securing a 'research exemption' and a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading tests.

District superintendents could facilitate curriculum reform by securing a 'research exemption' and a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading tests.

canonical_claim.text: The success of the American democratic experiment depends on preserving national unity as a community dedicated to common principles.

members.text: The success of the American democratic experiment depends on preserving national unity through shared principles and education.
The success of the American democratic experiment depends on preserving national unity as a community dedicated to common principles.

canonical_claim.text: The focus on individuality and a humane style of teaching inherited from progressive education has added to human happiness and should be preserved.

members.text: The humane style of teaching introduced by the progressive movement is a valuable insight that should be preserved.
The focus on individuality and a humane style of teaching inherited from progressive education has added to human happiness and should be preserved.

canonical_claim.text: To teach no values in a school curriculum is to evade an elemental duty and leads to societal rootlessness and incivility.

members.text: Failing to teach values leads to rootlessness and incivility in a society.
To teach no values in a school curriculum is to evade an elemental duty and leads to societal rootlessness and incivility.

canonical_claim.text: The Japanese science curriculum mandates that students learn the cardinal directions (east, west, north, and south) specifically in the context of the sun's movement.

members.text: The Japanese science curriculum mandates that students learn the cardinal directions (east, west, north, and south) specifically in the context of the sun's movement.
In Grade 3, the Japanese curriculum explicitly mandates teaching that the sun moves specifically from east to west.

canonical_claim.text: The Japanese Grade 5 science curriculum requires students to understand that the weight of water and a solute remains unchanged when the solute is dissolved.

members.text: The Japanese Grade 5 science curriculum requires students to understand that the weight of water and a solute remains unchanged when the solute is dissolved.
The Japanese curriculum requires Grade 5 students to learn that the polarity of an electromagnet changes when the direction of the electric current changes.

canonical_claim.text: The Japanese science curriculum specifies that Grade 5 students must learn the specific roles of sunlight, fertilizer, water, air, and temperature in plant growth and germination.

members.text: The Japanese science curriculum specifies that Grade 5 students must learn the specific roles of sunlight, fertilizer, water, air, and temperature in plant growth and germination.
The Japanese curriculum mandates that Grade 5 students study human growth inside the mother.

all_claims.text: Six major educational frustrations in the United States—over-testing, preschool fade-out, narrowed curriculum, low high school verbal scores, stagnant achievement gaps, and Common Core struggles—are caused by the reign of faulty ideas.
all_claims.source_passage: These problems have defied solution—but not primarily from lack of will or money, or from poverty or the shortcomings of teachers. They resist solution because they cannot be solved under the reign of the faulty ideas that caused them to arise.

all_claims.text: Changing underlying educational ideas is the most effective form of educational reform.
all_claims.source_passage: My long-standing view is that idea change will be the most effective educational reform of all.

all_claims.text: The achievement gap between social groups is primarily a knowledge gap and a language gap rather than a result of genetics or sociology.
all_claims.source_passage: Against the tide of sociological and genetic explanations of achievement gaps, the path-breaking work of researchers like John Guthrie, Betty Hart, and Todd Risley should have made it unnecessary to assert once more at this late date that the achievement gap is chiefly a knowledge gap and a language gap.

all_claims.text: Knowledge-based schooling can significantly ameliorate the achievement gap.
all_claims.source_passage: It can be greatly ameliorated by knowledge-based schooling.

all_claims.text: Parity of knowledge among young pupils should be understood as a civil right.
all_claims.source_passage: Once the centrality of knowledge (not general “skills”) is fully grasped by educators and the wider public, the right to parity of knowledge among young pupils will come to be understood as a civil right.

all_claims.text: Imparting the knowledge possessed by successful citizens is the only way to provide all children with the possibility of success.
all_claims.source_passage: This book continues my earlier theme that only by systematically imparting to all children the knowledge that is commonly possessed by successful citizens can all children gain the possibility of success

all_claims.text: Success in education should be defined as becoming an autonomous person who can communicate effectively with strangers, earn a living, and contribute to the community.
all_claims.source_passage: “success” understood as becoming a person with autonomy, who commands respect, has a communal voice that can write and speak effectively to strangers, can earn a good living, and can contribute to the wider community.

all_claims.text: Elementary schools must shift their primary goal from child-centered self-realization to community-centeredness.
all_claims.source_passage: The book has benefited from my clearer understanding that the key task facing our elementary schools is to shift our emphasis from the goal of self-realization to the goal of community—from child-centeredness to community-centeredness.

all_claims.text: The French elementary school system was historically characterized by an egalitarian uniformity where all children studied the same national curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: For many decades the French elementary school had been the pride and the terror of the young, with every child, rich or poor, having to undergo the very same rigors under the same national curriculum.

all_claims.text: The 1989 French education law (loi Jospin) replaced the uniform national curriculum with locally determined, individualized curriculums.
all_claims.source_passage: But in 1989, the bicentenary of the French Revolution, France passed a radical new education law—the loi Jospin—requiring all elementary schools to cease teaching the national curriculum and begin teaching locally determined curriculums, individualized further by a special emphasis in each school, called its projet.

all_claims.text: The 1989 French educational reform was prepared for by two decades of 'indoctrination' in American-style progressive education within French teacher training schools.
all_claims.source_passage: This drastic change had been silently prepared for during two decades of teacher indoctrination within French education schools into American-style progressive education.

all_claims.text: The 1989 French reforms prioritized general skills like 'critical thinking' over specific subject-matter knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: To compensate for all this novel heterogeneity, the unifying emphasis was to be on general skills such as “critical thinking” and “learning to learn.”

all_claims.text: In 1989, France passed the loi Jospin, which required elementary schools to replace the national curriculum with locally determined, individualized curricula.
all_claims.source_passage: But in 1989, the bicentenary of the French Revolution, France passed a radical new education law—the loi Jospin—requiring all elementary schools to cease teaching the national curriculum and begin teaching locally determined curriculums, individualized further by a special emphasis in each school, called its projet.

all_claims.text: The French shift to individualized curricula aimed to prioritize student individuality, native abilities, interests, and home culture.
all_claims.source_passage: The new law reflected those ideas: more attention was to be paid to the individuality of each student, to his or her native abilities, interests, and home culture.

all_claims.text: In response to increased curricular heterogeneity, the French system shifted its unifying focus to general skills like 'critical thinking' and 'learning to learn.'
all_claims.source_passage: To compensate for all this novel heterogeneity, the unifying emphasis was to be on general skills such as “critical thinking” and “learning to learn.”

all_claims.text: The 1989 French educational reform constituted a complete 'Americanization' of the French school system.
all_claims.source_passage: In other words, in 1989 the French decided to completely Americanize their school system overnight.

all_claims.text: The implementation of the loi Jospin created a valid natural experiment comparing communal, knowledge-centered schooling with child-centered, skill-centered schooling.
all_claims.source_passage: The sudden organizational change introduced by the new loi Jospin instituted a vast natural experiment. Which mode of schooling would work better and more fairly: the community-centered and knowledge-centered mode of the past, or the child-centered and skills-centered mode of the future?

all_claims.text: Key factors of French education—including teacher quality, budgets, and preschools—remained constant during the post-1989 curricular shift.
all_claims.source_passage: Teacher quality stayed the same by objective measures. School buildings and budgets did not change significantly. The superb French preschools were not covered by the new law, and stayed essentially the same.

all_claims.text: The primary cause of changes in French educational outcomes after 1989 was the change in curriculum and pedagogy.
all_claims.source_passage: The most decisive change was in the curriculum and pedagogy of the elementary school.

all_claims.text: Every demographic group in France experienced a steep decline in achievement following the 1989 educational reforms.
all_claims.source_passage: They reported an astonishingly steep decline in achievement in each demographic group—children from the homes of wealthy executives and professionals, children from the middle classes, children from various other well-­defined demographic groups including the unemployed.

all_claims.text: The post-1989 French educational system exacerbated social inequality, harming the most economically disadvantaged children the most.
all_claims.source_passage: Each group was academically harmed by the new system, and the harm became ever greater as one went down the economic scale. The children of the unemployed declined most of all. Achievement decreased. Inequality increased dramatically.

all_claims.text: The decline in French test scores among children of elite professionals proves that immigration is not the cause of the general educational decline in France.
all_claims.source_passage: The massive declines that occurred at the very top among children of white-collar workers and high-level professionals and executives cannot be blamed on the influx of North African immigrants, as some American experts are inclined to say.

all_claims.text: American education experts tend to blame immigrants for the French decline because they lack detailed knowledge of the French data.
all_claims.source_passage: Why are American education experts inclined to blame immigrants for the French decline? They know little about the details.

all_claims.text: French longitudinal studies provide a level of statistical confidence comparable to the American NAEP.
all_claims.source_passage: By those numbers alone, even without the refinements introduced by French and American experts, the two samplings yield very similar levels of confidence.

all_claims.text: Educational outcomes are primarily controlled by the dominance of specific ideas rather than by irresistible social forces.
all_claims.source_passage: These historical facts, coupled with recent cognitive research, will add credibility to the view that our educational fate is largely controlled by ideas rather than by irresistible social forces.

all_claims.text: The French educational decline after 1989 was a duplication of the American decline of the 1960s and 1970s, caused by a change in guiding ideas.
all_claims.source_passage: After 1989, the French in effect duplicated the American decline of the 1960s and 1970s by means of the same basic change in guiding ideas.

all_claims.text: American academic scores at all grade levels fell by more than 25 percent of a standard deviation between 1960 and 1980.
all_claims.source_passage: But between 1960 and 1980 American academic scores fell rapidly at all grade levels—more than 25 percent of a standard deviation, a big drop for large populations.

all_claims.text: The chief cause of the American educational decline was the nationwide adoption of inadequate ideas favoring individualistic development over communal education.
all_claims.source_passage: The chief cause of the decline was the nationwide adoption of a set of inadequate ideas. Though the ideas were partly true and beneficial, they were also partly incorrect and harmful because they neglected the communal dimension of education in favor of individualistic child-centered development.

all_claims.text: Early education should be appropriate to the child's age and nature as part of a natural developmental process.
all_claims.source_passage: Early education should be appropriate to the child’s age and nature, as part of a natural developmental process.

all_claims.text: Early education should be individualized to follow the learning styles and interests of each student.
all_claims.source_passage: Early education should be individualized as far as possible—to follow the learning styles and interests of each developing student.

all_claims.text: The unifying aim of education is to develop critical thinking and other general skills.
all_claims.source_passage: The unifying aim of education is to develop critical thinking and other general skills.

all_claims.text: Early education should be chiefly communal, focused on gaining proficiency in the language and conventions of the public sphere.
all_claims.source_passage: Early education should be chiefly communal—focused on gaining proficiency in the language and the conventions of the public sphere.

all_claims.text: Every child in a given locality should study basically the same early curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: Every child in each locality should study basically the same early curriculum.

all_claims.text: The unifying aim of early schooling is to impart to every child the enabling knowledge possessed by the most successful adults in the wider society.
all_claims.source_passage: The unifying aim of early schooling is autonomy and equality of opportunity: to impart to every child the enabling knowledge that is possessed by the most successful adults in the wider society.

all_claims.text: If a piece of knowledge is not inherently harmful for an advantaged child to know, it cannot be developmentally inappropriate for a disadvantaged child.
all_claims.source_passage: if an advantaged child at age seven knows certain things without harm, then it cannot be inherently harmful or "developmentally inappropriate" for a disadvantaged child also to know those very same things at the same age.

all_claims.text: Elementary school is a time for building socialization, which is the only means through which individuality can ultimately express itself.
all_claims.source_passage: Elementary school is a time for building socialization as the only means through which individuality can ultimately express itself.

all_claims.text: True individuality is achieved only through full membership in the 'tribe' via shared knowledge and conventions.
all_claims.source_passage: Only full membership in the tribe leads to individuality, as G. H. Mead profoundly observed.

all_claims.text: A communalist curriculum accommodates shared topics for each child, whereas a child-centered school devises different curriculum topics for different children.
all_claims.source_passage: And the communalist will further caution that there is a big distinction between accommodating shared curriculum topics for each child as the best schools do in the community-centered schools of Finland and Japan, and devising different curriculum topics for different children as we and the French now do in the child-centered school.

all_claims.text: Children must master shared conventions of standard language and social interaction to achieve full membership in the public sphere.
all_claims.source_passage: Children need to master the shared conventions of the standard language and of social interaction. They need to learn the shared knowledge and vocabulary of the nation, the shared spelling, pronunciation, and other conventions in the public sphere of the grown-up world.

all_claims.text: Only full membership in the social 'tribe' leads to the development of individuality.
all_claims.source_passage: Only full membership in the tribe leads to individuality, as G. H. Mead profoundly observed.

all_claims.text: Naturalistic and individualistic educational views have paradoxically turned schools into soulless test-prep factories.
all_claims.source_passage: But I will show in chapter 1 that, paradoxically, it is the naturalistic and individualistic view that has turned schools into soulless test-prep factories, with endless practice of strategies and skills, as they desperately attempt to overcome children’s lack of enabling knowledge—a lack partly induced by an individualized rather than a communal curriculum.

all_claims.text: Current educational assumptions lead to instruction that is not actually child-centered and perpetuates social inequality.
all_claims.source_passage: On the contrary, our assumptions about how children learn have led to instruction that is far from child centered, and that perpetuates inequality among children from different backgrounds.
