all_claims.text: Every child in a democracy deserves access to the shared, enabling knowledge and language of the de facto public commons.
all_claims.source_passage: The Core Knowledge Sequence has always been offered as just one exemplification of the more general idea that there exists a de facto public commons that enables our national language to be deployed effectively, and that every child in a democracy should have access to that shared, enabling knowledge and language.

all_claims.text: The general communal principle in education can be realized by different curricula that vary in their specific content.
all_claims.source_passage: The main mission of the Foundation is to serve that general communal idea, which can be realized by different curricula that vary in interesting ways.

all_claims.text: From 1975 to 1985, France possessed the highest achieving and most egalitarian school system of any large country in Europe.
all_claims.source_passage: No large nation has done so more successfully than France did from 1975 to 1985, when it had the highest achieving, most egalitarian school system of any large country in Europe.

all_claims.text: Earlier American school districts possessed a strong communal purposiveness before the shift in educational theory.
all_claims.source_passage: The Americans, of course, never had a national curriculum like the French, but the schools of most American districts did in earlier days have a strong communal purposiveness.

all_claims.text: Skill-centrism—the aim of imparting content-independent general skills—is a problematic and invalid educational goal.
all_claims.source_passage: But the third guiding idea, which one could call skill-centrism—the aim of imparting critical-thinking skills and similar general skills like problem solving—is altogether problematic.

all_claims.text: Naturalism and individualism in education stem from a theological belief that nature is a manifestation of a benevolent God and is therefore unerring.
all_claims.source_passage: Naturalism and individualism go together. They arose from a belief that nature, as the earthly manifestation of a beneficent God, is unerring and benign.

all_claims.text: The phrase 'providential individualism' describes the faith that letting educational affairs take a natural course will lead to optimal results.
all_claims.source_passage: I have adopted the phrase providential individualism to capture this point of view. I have found it useful in describing the widely held faith that if we let affairs take their natural course we are in the hands of a benign Providence, so all will be well, even optimal, in education.

all_claims.text: Empathetic and loving pedagogy is the best approach for young children, but it is not the exclusive property of naturalism or individualism.
all_claims.source_passage: Of course, naturalism and individualism have no monopoly on a loving and empathetic teaching, which is in all cases the best pedagogy for young children.

all_claims.text: The belief that early curriculum must be tailored to a child's individual abilities and temperament is a fatal weakness of American education.
all_claims.source_passage: Naturalism and individualism... have also been taken to imply—and this is a fatal weakness—a curriculum that arises from the child’s individual abilities and temperament.

all_claims.text: Equating early education with the metaphor of individual 'development' is misleading and unsupported by developmental psychology.
all_claims.source_passage: I will argue, with support from developmental psychology, that equating early education with the metaphor of individual “development” is misleading... To make the emphases and content of the child’s early schooling largely dependent upon the child’s uniqueness is an idea unsupported by developmental psychology.

all_claims.text: Social impositions in schooling are natural occurrences despite being labeled 'unnatural' by developmentalists.
all_claims.source_passage: so-called “unnatural” social impositions are the most natural things in the world

all_claims.text: School systems with 'lockstep' or uniform curricula, such as Finland and Japan, produce effective results and happy students.
all_claims.source_passage: school systems with so-called “lockstep” curricula in the early grades (Finland, Japan) have very child-happy effective schools that score near the top in international studies.

all_claims.text: A differentiated curriculum is harmful to student achievement and educational equity.
all_claims.source_passage: Indeed, international studies have shown that a differentiated curriculum is harmful to achievement and equity.

all_claims.text: There is weak to nonexistent scientific evidence supporting the existence of individual 'learning styles.'
all_claims.source_passage: The evidence for individual learning styles is weak to nonexistent.

all_claims.text: Individualizing the elementary classroom inevitably leads to the fragmentation of the school curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: And in practice the individualizing of the elementary-school classroom has led to fragmentation of the curriculum.

all_claims.text: The doctrine of 'general skills' (like critical thinking) is used as a defense for fragmented and individualized curricula.
all_claims.source_passage: This fragmentation is defended and supposedly turned to benefit by a third doctrine: that the goal of education is the imparting of general skills like critical thinking, creative thinking, problem solving, and cooperative thinking.

all_claims.text: Educational individualism logically requires the existence of general, content-independent skills to maintain educational unity.
all_claims.source_passage: Educational individualism has always required the general-skills idea. To make thinking skill the ultimate goal renders irrelevant the fragmenting of school topics that must occur when the teacher is urged to tailor the curriculum to the uniqueness of each child.

all_claims.text: Modern cognitive science has refuted the idea that students can be trained in content-independent thinking skills.
all_claims.source_passage: But reality has not accepted this hopeful idea about skills, and recent cognitive science has been fatal to it.

all_claims.text: John Dewey proposed that scientific 'habits of thought' could serve as a unifying principle to prevent individualized schooling from becoming mere distraction.
all_claims.source_passage: Dewey is right about the structure of the difficulty... Dewey says, by making critical thinking rather than mere facts the proper goal of child-centered education.

all_claims.text: Few people in American education are willing to challenge the prevailing emphasis on individualism due to its political sensitivity.
all_claims.source_passage: he has also identified what may be the most recalcitrant political problem in American education—that few dare challenge our emphasis on individualism.

all_claims.text: The term 'expertise' is more accurate than 'skills' because expertise inherently fuses knowledge and formal ability.
all_claims.source_passage: It is a far more accurate term than skills because expertise implies an inherent fusion of form and content, knowledge and skill that can’t easily be dissociated in the human mind.

all_claims.text: Broad subject-matter knowledge serves as the foundation for problem-solving abilities.
all_claims.source_passage: broad subject-matter knowledge is the basis of problem-solving abilities.

all_claims.text: If instruction is individualized 'in mass,' a further principle is required to guide instruction and provide unity to the student's experience.
all_claims.source_passage: if schooling is to be truly individualized and the students are no longer to be taught 'in mass,' then some further principle is needed to guide instruction and lend unity to the experience of the individual student.

all_claims.text: Critical thinking, rather than facts, is the principle Dewey proposed to unify child-centered education.
all_claims.source_passage: That can only be accomplished, Dewey says, by making critical thinking rather than mere facts the proper goal of child-centered education.

all_claims.text: The refusal to challenge individualism is one of the most persistent political problems in American education.
all_claims.source_passage: he has also identified what may be the most recalcitrant political problem in American education—that few dare challenge our emphasis on individualism.

all_claims.text: The idea that critical thinking can unify individualized schooling was based on scientifically incorrect views of skill development.
all_claims.source_passage: The proposal that critical thinking is an aim that unifies fragmented and individualized schooling made sense in Dewey’s era, when scientists had incorrect ideas about skill development.

all_claims.text: General cognitive abilities such as learning and reasoning cannot be studied or developed independently of specific content domains.
all_claims.source_passage: Research clearly rejects the classical views on human cognition in which general abilities such as learning, reasoning, problem solving, and concept formation correspond to capacities and abilities that can be studied independently of the content domains.

all_claims.text: Skills are intrinsically tied to particular content domains, a principle known as domain specificity.
all_claims.source_passage: Modern cognitive psychology holds that the skills that are to be imparted to a child by the school are intrinsically tied to particular content domains. This is called the domain specificity of skills.

all_claims.text: Thinking skills cannot be easily transferred from one subject matter to another.
all_claims.source_passage: Thinking skills cannot readily be separated from one subject matter and applied to other subject matters.

all_claims.text: The term 'expertise' is a more accurate description of educational goals than 'skill' because it implies the necessity of knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: Think of how significantly our view of schooling might change if suddenly policy makers, instead of using the term skill, had to use the more accurate, knowledge-drenched term expertise.

all_claims.text: A single, overarching general skill does not exist.
all_claims.source_passage: Yet that single, overarching skill doesn’t exist. Believing in that mirage has actually resulted in the 'distraction' Dewey feared.

all_claims.text: The combination of child-centeredness and the faulty theory of general skills has led to an education system dominated by soul-deadening drills.
all_claims.source_passage: A benign child-centeredness coupled with a faulty theory about general skills has led us to a child purgatory of skill drills.

all_claims.text: Skill drills have failed to improve both actual skills and standardized test scores.
all_claims.source_passage: These have produced neither good skills nor good scores on the ever-looming tests.

all_claims.text: No sensible person would disparage either the goal of child-centered self-realization or the goal of community-centeredness.
all_claims.source_passage: No sensible person would disparage either goal. But the emphasis must shift decisively for the sake of the community and the individual child.

all_claims.text: The verbal scores of American seventeen-year-olds have stayed low and largely unchanged since the National Assessment of Educational Progress (NAEP) began recording them in 1971.
all_claims.source_passage: The verbal scores of our seventeen-year-olds have stayed low and largely unchanged ever since NAEP began recording them in 1971.

all_claims.text: The education gap between Black and White students in the United States narrowed steadily until recent decades.
all_claims.source_passage: Moreover, as John Bishop has long pointed out, the education gap between blacks and whites had narrowed steadily until recent decades.

all_claims.text: While educational historians correctly reject the notion of a 'golden age,' they concede that a massive test score decline occurred between 1960 and 1980.
all_claims.source_passage: The belief that there was once a golden age of American education is scorned by educational historians. They are of course right. But they concede that there was indeed a large test score decline—over a quarter of a standard deviation—in grade school and high school test scores between 1960 and 1980.

all_claims.text: If a piece of knowledge is not inherently harmful for an advantaged seven-year-old to know, it cannot be logically classified as 'developmentally inappropriate' for a disadvantaged child of the same age.
all_claims.source_passage: foes of inequality like me caution that if an advantaged child at age seven knows certain things without harm, then it cannot be inherently harmful or 'developmentally inappropriate' for a disadvantaged child also to know those very same things at the same age.

all_claims.text: Naturalists in education concede that communal knowledge is important, just as communalists concede that natural development cannot be thwarted.
all_claims.source_passage: Naturalists will of course concede that communal knowledge is important, while communalists will concede that nature cannot be thwarted.

all_claims.text: The primary objective of this book is to promote the general communal principle of education rather than to advocate for any specific curriculum product like the Core Knowledge Sequence.
all_claims.source_passage: But the promotion of any single curriculum guide has been far from my mind, and is not a motivation for this book. Rather, this book's aim is to promote the general communal principle.

all_claims.text: The core of educational 'naturalism' and 'individualism' is the theological assumption that a benevolent purpose is inherent in Nature and will guarantee optimal outcomes if left to take its course.
all_claims.source_passage: The source of this faith is the unspoken assumption that a benevolent purpose is present in Nature, and will assure a beneficent result.

all_claims.text: The adoption of critical thinking as an educational goal was a practical necessity intended to provide unity to a curriculum that had been fragmented by individualized instruction.
all_claims.source_passage: He is stating a practical necessity: if the content of the curriculum is to be scattered and diversified by 'dealing with pupils individually and not in mass,' then some further principle is needed to guide instruction and lend unity to the experience of the individual student. That can only be accomplished, Dewey says, by making critical thinking rather than mere facts the proper goal of child-centered education.

all_claims.text: The French longitudinal studies (based on 4,200 students from a cohort of 1 million) yield statistical confidence levels similar to the American NAEP (based on 8,700 students from 6 million).
all_claims.source_passage: The French longitudinal studies are based on a sample size of forty-two hundred students from an age cohort of one million. By those numbers alone... the two samplings yield very similar levels of confidence.

all_claims.text: The only way to make standardized tests fair and productive is to base them on well-defined, knowledge-based curriculums.
all_claims.source_passage: There’s just one way to do that—to base them on well-defined, knowledge-based curriculums. There is no other way of making tests fair and productive.

all_claims.text: The average reading score of graduating seventeen-year-olds is a valid single measure of the academic quality of a school system.
all_claims.source_passage: If one had to choose a single measure of the academic quality of a school system, the average reading score of its graduating seventeen-year-olds would serve.

all_claims.text: Verbal scores at age seventeen predict college and career readiness as well as later economic success.
all_claims.source_passage: Verbal scores at that age predict students’ college and career readiness and their later economic success.

all_claims.text: A reading test is effectively a test of general knowledge and vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: A reading test is a test of general knowledge and vocabulary; it gauges a student’s ability to operate effectively within the public sphere.

all_claims.text: Any educational policy that fails to improve reading test scores is a failed policy because valid reading tests measure a student's initiation into the public sphere.
all_claims.source_passage: Since a valid reading test probes a student’s degree of initiation into the public sphere—a fundamental aim of education—any policy that lowers or neglects to improve test scores in reading is a failed educational policy.

all_claims.text: American reading scores for seventeen-year-olds declined significantly between 1988 and 2012.
all_claims.source_passage: For, in 2012, a decade after the test-intensive No Child Left Behind Act (NCLB) went into effect, the reading scores of seventeen-year-olds came in significantly lower than they had been in 1988 before NCLB was enacted.

all_claims.text: The cohort that attained the highest recorded age-13 NAEP reading scores went on to show lower scores at age 17.
all_claims.source_passage: This 2012 result for seventeen-year-olds seems particularly anomalous, because that same cohort four years earlier, at age thirteen, had attained the topmost score that NAEP had ever recorded—higher than any prior cohort except 1992, with which it was tied.

all_claims.text: High-stakes testing regimens under NCLB deeply affected the content taught by schools and teachers in early grades.
all_claims.source_passage: Those testing regimens will have deeply affected what schools and teachers taught them in their early grades.

all_claims.text: Intensive test preparation in early grades does not help long-term mature reading ability.
all_claims.source_passage: Yet all that intensive test prep did not, in the end, help their mature reading.

all_claims.text: NCLB-era testing regimens helped improve the mechanics of early reading, such as decoding fluency and accuracy.
all_claims.source_passage: That’s a troubling thought to me, since the testing regimens have clearly helped improve the mechanics of early reading (that is, the ability to sound out fast and effortlessly)—an important gain.

all_claims.text: The narrowing of reading achievement gaps in early grades is due to improved fluency scores, not long-term literacy gains.
all_claims.source_passage: Because of that the reading gaps between ethnic and racial groups have narrowed in those years, when fluency is an important part of the score. But, alas, those equity gaps begin widening again between ages thirteen and seventeen, when knowledge and vocabulary are decisive.

all_claims.text: Vocabulary size is determined by the cumulative knowledge acquired from birth to age seventeen, not by high school instruction alone.
all_claims.source_passage: The vocabulary size of seventeen-year-olds is not determined at age seventeen, or sixteen, or fifteen. It’s a plant of slow growth that is determined by the knowledge that has been gradually acquired from a child’s overall experience from birth to age seventeen.

all_claims.text: Early schooling is a critical factor in vocabulary growth, particularly for disadvantaged children.
all_claims.source_passage: Early schooling can play a major role in vocabulary growth, especially for disadvantaged students.

all_claims.text: The No Child Left Behind Act (NCLB) improved the mechanics of early reading, specifically the ability to sound out words effortlessly.
all_claims.source_passage: Younger students can now decode texts with more fluency and accuracy than they did before NCLB.

all_claims.text: Reading gaps between ethnic and racial groups narrow in early years when fluency is the primary component of test scores.
all_claims.source_passage: Because of that the reading gaps between ethnic and racial groups have narrowed in those years, when fluency is an important part of the score.

all_claims.text: Reading equity gaps widen significantly between ages thirteen and seventeen when vocabulary and knowledge become the decisive factors in comprehension.
all_claims.source_passage: But, alas, those equity gaps begin widening again between ages thirteen and seventeen, when knowledge and vocabulary are decisive.

all_claims.text: High schools are not responsible for the decline in reading performance or the widening of the gap in older students.
all_claims.source_passage: One might first be inclined to blame the high schools for undoing what the grade schools had achieved in reading. But as a specialist in reading comprehension I know that such an accusation is incorrect.

all_claims.text: A seventeen-year-old's vocabulary size is a cumulative result of knowledge acquired gradually from birth.
all_claims.source_passage: It’s a plant of slow growth that is determined by the knowledge that has been gradually acquired from a child’s overall experience from birth to age seventeen.

all_claims.text: Current early schooling practices have inadvertently depressed the vocabulary growth of students by age seventeen.
all_claims.source_passage: While current schooling in early grades has improved the sounding-out aspects of reading, we can infer from the NAEP results that it has depressed the vocabulary sizes of seventeen-year-olds.

all_claims.text: Schools are spending excessive time on test preparation and insufficient time on the wide knowledge acquisition required for vocabulary growth.
all_claims.source_passage: My conclusion, which sets the stage for this chapter, is that too much time is now being spent on test preparation in early grades, and too little time is being spent on gaining the wide knowledge required for a broad vocabulary.

all_claims.text: The decline in American public schooling quality began in the 1960s and 1970s.
all_claims.source_passage: That report showed that American public schooling had declined in quality during the 1960s and 1970s.

all_claims.text: High-stakes testing has caused schools to abandon the teaching of history and the arts in favor of formal 'reading' instruction.
all_claims.source_passage: a number of schools told me, after high-stakes reading tests came into effect under NCLB, that they could no longer teach history and the arts. They now were being made to teach 'reading' instead, with a strong emphasis on test preparation.

all_claims.text: Educational administrators incorrectly believe that drills in formal skills like 'making inferences' are more effective for raising scores than learning content like history or science.
all_claims.source_passage: Their higher-ups were under the impression that intense classes devoted to 'making inferences' and 'finding the main idea' would improve reading scores more effectively than learning about ancient Egypt or the solar system

all_claims.text: Misguided efforts to raise test scores through skills-based instruction are likely the cause of low scores among seventeen-year-olds.
all_claims.source_passage: By an iron law of unintended consequences, the low scores of seventeen-year olds were probably caused by misguided, time-consuming efforts to raise scores.

all_claims.text: Common Core State Standards tests will continue to depress reading competence unless schooling prioritizes knowledge acquisition in early grades.
all_claims.source_passage: This chapter will predict that the high-stakes reading tests that accompany the new Common Core State Standards are going to have a similar nugatory or depressing effect on the reading competence of our seventeen-year-olds of the future

all_claims.text: Excessive and poorly designed testing drains creative approaches from classrooms and takes time away from actual learning.
all_claims.source_passage: Done poorly, in excess, or without clear purpose, [tests] take valuable time away from teaching and learning, draining creative approaches from our classrooms.

all_claims.text: Formal comprehension skill drills, such as 'summarizing' or 'questioning the author,' provide only an initial benefit that quickly plateaus.
all_claims.source_passage: And indeed students do show an initial positive effect from practicing finding the main idea. But their progress quickly reaches a limit and then halts.

all_claims.text: Drills in formal comprehension skills have failed to raise mature reading scores.
all_claims.source_passage: Drills in formal comprehension skills have not raised mature reading scores; rather, they have taken up a lot of class time that could have been devoted to knowledge building.

all_claims.text: Schooling has been made boring and ineffective by test-prep exercises in formal skills like summarizing and questioning the author.
all_claims.source_passage: In language arts, for instance, schooling has been bent out of shape and made boring and ineffective by test-prep exercises in skills like “summarizing” and “questioning the author.”

all_claims.text: Students show an initial positive effect from practicing main-idea finding, but their progress quickly reaches a limit and halts.
all_claims.source_passage: And indeed students do show an initial positive effect from practicing finding the main idea. But their progress quickly reaches a limit and then halts.

all_claims.text: The emphasis on formal technique over subject-matter knowledge in early grades produces 17-year-olds who lack the vocabulary to understand mature public discourse.
all_claims.source_passage: This emphasis on technique at the expense of building subject-matter knowledge in early grades produces students who at age seventeen lack the knowledge and vocabulary to understand the mature language of newspapers, textbooks, and political speeches.

all_claims.text: Reading comprehension in any given case depends more on relevant knowledge than on formal strategies.
all_claims.source_passage: We know that the skill of reading comprehension in any given case depends more on relevant knowledge than on formal strategies.

all_claims.text: Reading strategies, once briefly learned, take care of themselves.
all_claims.source_passage: Once briefly learned, the strategies will take care of themselves.

all_claims.text: After decoding is mastered, relevant knowledge is the skill set that most reliably determines reading comprehension.
all_claims.source_passage: The basic principle for schools to keep in mind is that, once decoding has been mastered, the “skill set” that most reliably determines reading comprehension is relevant knowledge.

all_claims.text: The unpredictability of topics on current reading tests forces schools to focus on strategies and meaning-guessing rather than systematic knowledge acquisition.
all_claims.source_passage: The unpredictability of the test topics forces the schools to stress the externalities of test taking and of meaning guessing.

all_claims.text: Well-established reading tests like the Gates-MacGinitie and NAEP are technically reliable and valid measures of average reading ability.
all_claims.source_passage: Despite their indifference to the school curriculum, the better reading tests are technically reliable and valid. The Gates-MacGinitie reading test, the Iowa Test of Basic Skills, and the National Assessment of Educational Progress are good measures of the average reading abilities of large groups of students.

all_claims.text: Current standardized reading tests are consequentially invalid because they lead schools to engage in self-defeating educational practices.
all_claims.source_passage: to engage in self-defeating practices. They are consequentially invalid.

all_claims.text: The idea that there is a generalized, transferable 'main-idea-finding skill' is a myth.
all_claims.source_passage: By focusing on main-idea finding, the standards promote the myth that there is a generalized main-idea-finding skill, which if practiced and developed will enhance reading ability.

all_claims.text: Reading test questions about main ideas and inference-making are actually probing knowledge and vocabulary, not general strategies.
all_claims.source_passage: The test questions about main ideas and inference making imply the misleading message that they are probing all-purpose strategies and skills of predicting, summarizing, and “inferencing.” But they are doing no such thing. The tests are probing knowledge and vocabulary.

all_claims.text: The defect in modern testing lies primarily in the scientific shortcomings of content-free state standards rather than the tests themselves.
all_claims.source_passage: That defect lies less in the tests themselves than in the scientific shortcomings of the empty state standards on which they are based.

all_claims.text: Educational standards have misled schools regarding the actual nature of reading skill.
all_claims.source_passage: The standards have misled the schools regarding the nature of reading skill.

all_claims.text: Current student performance on tests disproves the existence of a transferable main-idea skill, as students heavily drilled in this skill are not seeing improved outcomes.
all_claims.source_passage: But if that were so for mature reading ability, the current generation of students would be performing better on the tests, for they have all been well schooled in main-idea finding.

all_claims.text: Standardized test questions regarding main ideas and inference do not actually probe all-purpose strategies like predicting or summarizing.
all_claims.source_passage: The test questions about main ideas and inference making imply the misleading message that they are probing all-purpose strategies and skills of predicting, summarizing, and “inferencing.” But they are doing no such thing.

all_claims.text: Standardized reading tests are actually probes of a student's knowledge and vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: The tests are probing knowledge and vocabulary.

all_claims.text: The No Child Left Behind Act's emphasis on decoding has been productive for improving the mechanics of early reading.
all_claims.source_passage: To the credit of the No Child Left Behind Act, the new focus on decoding has been highly productive for the mechanics of early reading.

all_claims.text: After a student masters decoding and attains fluency, relevant knowledge becomes the primary component of reading skill.
all_claims.source_passage: But once decoding has been mastered and fluency attained, relevant knowledge becomes the chief component of reading skill.

all_claims.text: Test makers imply a lie by suggesting their questions probe formal skills when they actually reward relevant vocabulary and knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: No doubt unintentionally, and with inadequate knowledge of psycholinguistics, the test makers are implying a lie. By the form of their questions they suggest that they are probing formal skills. But, no matter how well trained students become in main-idea finding, the student with the smaller relevant vocabulary and knowledge is the one who will fare worse on the test.

all_claims.text: The 'opt-out' protest movement indicates that parents and teachers have correctly identified that the current testing system is failing.
all_claims.source_passage: That parents and teachers alike are demonstrating against the new tests, and opting out of them, is a pretty good indication that they have correctly concluded that something has gone wrong.

all_claims.text: Current test preparation narrows elementary schooling by displacing subjects like social studies, science, and the arts.
all_claims.source_passage: Teachers have complained that test preparation has narrowed elementary schooling—pushing out social studies, science, and the arts.

all_claims.text: Constant testing has placed unproductive stress on children and teenagers, leading to health and psychological issues.
all_claims.source_passage: Parents have complained of the neglect of history and the arts, adding that constant testing has placed unfruitful stress upon their children.

all_claims.text: State math standards are superior to reading standards because math standards are content-specific and build on each other progressively.
all_claims.source_passage: Current math standards are much better guides for teachers and test makers than are current standards in reading.

all_claims.text: American standards makers have avoided specifying content in reading due to fear of political controversy.
all_claims.source_passage: The makers of standards have decided that while it is politically feasible to create a definite content guide in math, fierce controversy would follow if they created a definite content guide in reading.

all_claims.text: Reading standards in states like Texas and Virginia are repetitive and 'content empty,' failing to provide a clear progression of topics across grades.
all_claims.source_passage: My own state of Virginia is more forthright about the inherent repetitiousness and content emptiness of its reading standard

all_claims.text: State educational standards focus on the skill of finding the main idea rather than specifying topics or texts.
all_claims.source_passage: instead of specifying either topics or texts, the standards makers focus on the skill of finding the main idea.

all_claims.text: The reading standards in Virginia are repetitious and lack substantive content across grade levels.
all_claims.source_passage: My own state of Virginia is more forthright about the inherent repetitiousness and content emptiness of its reading standards

all_claims.text: The Common Core State Standards in English Language Arts lack real progression in content across grade levels.
all_claims.source_passage: Nor are the new Common Core State Standards in English language arts exempt from this same lack of real progression or content

all_claims.text: A student's ability to identify a main idea on a test is a direct result of their understanding of the specific passage, not a transferable skill.
all_claims.source_passage: If readers understand a passage, they will reliably answer the test question about the main idea. If they don’t understand the passage, they won’t.

all_claims.text: Complex writing often contains multiple central ideas rather than a single 'main idea' as suggested by tests.
all_claims.source_passage: Moreover, in good, complex writing there isn’t usually a single main idea.

all_claims.text: Empty skills-based standards were created primarily for political expediency.
all_claims.source_passage: These empty standards were created out of political expediency. The makers of standards and tests have built up an artificial construct, politically painless for the makers of standards and of tests

all_claims.text: Reading comprehension is not an all-purpose skill like decoding.
all_claims.source_passage: Test makers have dutifully followed the standards makers, presenting reading comprehension as an all-purpose skill like decoding. Since it isn’t, the current standards and tests have created a fictional alternative universe in our classrooms.

all_claims.text: The ability to comprehend a text is dependent on having relevant knowledge and vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: In the real world, an ability to comprehend a piece of writing depends on one’s having the knowledge and vocabulary relevant to that passage.

all_claims.text: A reading test is an unfair measure of schooling if the school has not taught the specific knowledge required to understand the test passages.
all_claims.source_passage: If the school does not teach students the knowledge and vocabulary they need to understand the passages on the test, then the test is unfair as a measure of what the school has successfully taught.

all_claims.text: Current standardized reading tests primarily reflect knowledge students acquired outside of school.
all_claims.source_passage: The test simply reflects the knowledge and vocabulary that students have picked up from all sources.

all_claims.text: Test makers define reading as a set of 'strategies' to give the appearance of fairness in the absence of a shared curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: a method has had to be devised that seems to make these inherently unfair tests fair. That method has been to define reading ability as a set of strategies, and then to create test items that appear to probe those strategies.

all_claims.text: The phrasing of test questions is misleadingly designed to suggest they test strategy expertise rather than specific content knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: Their form indicates misleadingly that strategy expertise rather than specific knowledge is being probed.

all_claims.text: Texas standardized test items misleadingly indicate that strategy expertise rather than specific knowledge is being probed.
all_claims.source_passage: Their form indicates misleadingly that strategy expertise rather than specific knowledge is being probed.

all_claims.text: Reading comprehension test items are easy for students who understand the passage but impossible for those who do not, regardless of strategy training.
all_claims.source_passage: Answering such items is easy for students who understand the passages, but not for those who don’t—no matter how many drills in comprehension strategies the students had before the test.

all_claims.text: Relevant domain-specific background knowledge enables a student to understand a passage and answer questions fast without conscious strategizing.
all_claims.source_passage: A child who has the relevant domain-specific background knowledge will understand the passage and get the right answer fast, without conscious strategizing.

all_claims.text: A student lacking background knowledge must rely on conscious strategies and glosses, which prevents them from finishing the test and leads to errors.
all_claims.source_passage: A child who does not have enough relevant knowledge will have to use special glosses in the test and consciously apply strategies; that child won’t finish the test and will get many answers wrong.

all_claims.text: The educational system has devised a fictitious alternative world where metaskills are presented as being as important as knowledge and vocabulary to mask test unfairness.
all_claims.source_passage: In sum, to mask the inherent unfairness of these tests, a fictitious alternative world has been devised in which metaskills look as important as knowledge and vocabulary.

all_claims.text: Content-evading state standards define the reading curriculum as a set of all-purpose techniques like inference making and predicting.
all_claims.source_passage: The substance of the reading curriculum had been described in the content-evading state standards as consisting of all-purpose techniques like inference making, predicting, and main-idea finding.

all_claims.text: Standardized reading tests appear fair because they are 'standards-based,' even though they are inherently unfair to disadvantaged students.
all_claims.source_passage: Despite their unfairness (especially to disadvantaged students), they have thus been made to appear to be fair.

all_claims.text: Instruction in reading strategies offers a small initial score boost but reaches a plateau quickly and does not require ongoing practice.
all_claims.source_passage: Lessons in reading strategies offer an initial score boost for test taking, but are quickly learned; they plateau fast, and they don’t have to be practiced.

all_claims.text: The optimal amount of time to spend on reading comprehension strategy instruction is approximately two weeks.
all_claims.source_passage: Two weeks on comprehension strategies is optimal. There is no practical utility after that.

all_claims.text: Forcing students to become self-conscious about applying reading strategies distracts them and degrades their actual reading performance.
all_claims.source_passage: Worse, making young students become highly self-conscious about applying strategies distracts their attention and degrades their performance.

all_claims.text: Reading instruction and testing should be restructured to resemble math, with specific content standards and tests based on that content.
all_claims.source_passage: The structure will need to become more like that of math, with specific content in the standards and the curriculum, and with tests based on that content.

all_claims.text: General skills such as close reading, complexity managing, and main-idea finding do not actually exist as transferable skills.
all_claims.source_passage: high-stakes reading tests that, as before, pretend to test the general skills of close reading, complexity managing, and main-idea finding—general skills that do not exist.

all_claims.text: The most important words in the Common Core standards are those stating that standards can only be implemented through a curriculum structured to develop rich content knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: They contain the welcome admonition that the standards can only be properly implemented through a “curriculum intentionally and coherently structured to develop rich content knowledge within and across grades.” Those precious words are to my mind the most important ones in the new standards.

all_claims.text: Common Core reading tests are unlikely to be more educationally productive than previous tests because they still lack grade-by-grade content guidance.
all_claims.source_passage: In the absence of specific grade-by-grade content guidance, the makers of the Common Core tests are placed in the same position as the makers of the previous tests... it will be hard for them to be any more educationally productive than the tests they are displacing.

all_claims.text: New reading tests based on state standards will not be any more educationally productive than the tests they are displacing.
all_claims.source_passage: So, despite the expense and computerized novelty of the new reading tests, it will be hard for them to be any more educationally productive than the tests they are displacing.

all_claims.text: The Common Core standards for main-idea finding and close reading are the most content-free, strategy-like elements of the framework.
all_claims.source_passage: Those are the Common Core standards that are the most content-free, strategy-like elements.

all_claims.text: Prior relevant knowledge of words and topics is the only way to successfully answer test questions that probe comprehension and inference.
all_claims.source_passage: the secret to answering such questions will not be hours of practice of 'inferencing skills' and 'close-reading skills,' but can only be answered through the student’s prior relevant knowledge of the words and the topics.

all_claims.text: Logical acumen cannot decide the meaning of a word when the context allows for multiple correct interpretations.
all_claims.source_passage: Anyway, logical acumen will not decide that particular issue.

all_claims.text: Current standardized reading tests are based on a wrong theory of inference and reading comprehension.
all_claims.source_passage: It’s based on a wrong theory of inference and reading comprehension.

all_claims.text: Inferences in reading tend to be of the part-whole situation model rather than the deductive-syllogistic model.
all_claims.source_passage: inferences tend to be of the part-whole situation model, not the deductive-syllogistic model.

all_claims.text: Knowledge-based schooling is the most promising way to narrow the reading gap between demographic groups.
all_claims.source_passage: It is by far the most promising way to... narrow the reading gap between demographic groups.

all_claims.text: Classroom exercises focused on general reading skills are knowledge-displacing, soul-deadening, and useless after ten lessons.
all_claims.source_passage: to replace exercises that are knowledge-displacing, soul-deadening, and essentially useless after ten lessons.

all_claims.text: Substantive knowledge gains constitute the most effective long-range strategy for reading comprehension.
all_claims.source_passage: When a student gains a real advance in substantive knowledge, it’s the best long-range comprehension strategy.

all_claims.text: Accountability in schools requires standards that are specific guides to curricular content.
all_claims.source_passage: Only standards that are guides to curricular content can be foundations for fair and productive tests.

all_claims.text: The meaning of phrases like 'remaining elements' in scientific contexts requires specific background knowledge that fifth graders typically lack.
all_claims.source_passage: Its meaning requires specific background knowledge of a highly sophisticated kind (probably already provided earlier in the source), which most fifth graders will normally lack.

all_claims.text: Time currently spent on skill-based reading exercises should instead be spent on subject matters that build knowledge and vocabulary to induce reading competence.
all_claims.source_passage: Those wasted hours ought to be spent on far more interesting and rewarding subject matters that will build up knowledge and vocabulary, and therefore induce greater reading competence.

all_claims.text: Current educational standards lack the specificity and insight into the nature of reading required for meaningful accountability.
all_claims.source_passage: The standards have not told them with adequate specificity or adequate insight into the actual nature of reading.

all_claims.text: Standards must serve as guides to specific curricular content to function as a foundation for fair and productive tests.
all_claims.source_passage: Only standards that are guides to curricular content can be foundations for fair and productive tests.

all_claims.text: Fair and productive accountability in reading is impossible without definite content standards.
all_claims.source_passage: But they must come to understand that without definite content standards, there can be no fair and productive accountability in reading.

all_claims.text: Authorities have a responsibility to state exactly what children need to learn and schools need to teach in every grade.
all_claims.source_passage: The authorities can’t just wave their hands and imperiously demand better reading without stating what children need to learn and schools need to teach in a given grade in order to gradually reach that long-range goal.

all_claims.text: Punitive threats to teachers attached to current reading tests should be removed as an intermediate policy step.
all_claims.source_passage: Meanwhile, a positive intermediate step would be to remove the punitive threats to teachers attached to our educationally invalid reading tests—the subject of the next chapter.

all_claims.text: The removal of high-stakes pressure would allow educators to focus on the long-term acquisition of knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: If the high stakes were removed, sensible teachers and principals would be willing to pay more attention to the long arc of knowledge acquisition, which is the route to producing good readers and competent high school graduates.

all_claims.text: Current reading tests make fraudulent claims when used to measure what schools have actually taught.
all_claims.source_passage: To sum up: This chapter has shown that recent, technically valid reading tests make fraudulent claims if they are used to gauge what the schools have taught.

all_claims.text: Existing reading tests are consequentially invalid because they do more harm than good and narrow the focus of schooling.
all_claims.source_passage: They are “consequentially invalid.” They do more harm than good.

all_claims.text: Topic familiarity is a stronger predictor of reading comprehension than the formal ability to handle difficult syntax.
all_claims.source_passage: (This result anticipated recent work showing that topic familiarity, often indicated by word familiarity, is a stronger predictor of comprehension than formal ability to deal with difficult syntax.)

all_claims.text: Initiation into the public sphere is a fundamental aim of education.
all_claims.source_passage: Since a valid reading test probes a student’s degree of initiation into the public sphere—a fundamental aim of education—any policy that lowers or neglects to improve test scores in reading is a failed educational policy.

all_claims.text: Following the education summits of 1989 and 1996, every US state except Iowa established grade-by-grade reading standards.
all_claims.source_passage: After the governors’ education summits of 1989 and 1996, all but one state (Iowa) developed grade-by-grade reading standards on which schooling in the state would be based.

all_claims.text: The improvement in teaching decoding led to improved comprehension specifically for simple, undemanding texts in early grades.
all_claims.source_passage: I mentioned one consequence of these reforms: an improvement in the teaching of decoding... That in turn should also have produced an improvement in reading comprehension, and it did so for undemanding texts on everyday topics in the early grades.

all_claims.text: Students in large American cities take approximately eight standardized tests per year, which consumes over 4 percent of total school time.
all_claims.source_passage: an alarming study recently produced by the Council of Great City Schools reporting that each student in the large cities now takes about eight standardized tests in a year. Just sitting for the tests takes over 4 percent of school time.

all_claims.text: The unpredictability of test topics is an inherent and necessary feature of all-purpose reading tests when the school curriculum is not standardized.
all_claims.source_passage: That is an expected, even obvious, characteristic of an all-purpose sort of reading test, which is, of course, the only sort that could possibly be fair when the topics of the school curriculum are unknown.

all_claims.text: Standardized tests such as the Gates-MacGinitie and Iowa tests demonstrate high internal reliability with correlation scores of approximately 0.9.
all_claims.source_passage: Gates and Iowa each show strong internal correlations between different forms of the same test (about .9 on a scale in which 1 is perfect).

all_claims.text: There is a scientific consensus among cognitive scientists that relevant knowledge is the primary driver of reading comprehension once decoding is mastered.
all_claims.source_passage: But once decoding has been mastered and fluency attained, relevant knowledge becomes the chief component of reading skill. Every cognitive scientist specializing in the subject would agree with that statement.

all_claims.text: Parents should take an active role in demanding that schools replace 'knowledge-displacing' skill exercises with substantive content in history, science, and the arts.
all_claims.source_passage: A positive aim of parents should be to demand knowledge-building substance in their child’s language arts classroom, to replace exercises that are knowledge-displacing, soul-deadening, and essentially useless after ten lessons.

all_claims.text: Accountability proponents have a duty to join with local stakeholders to define the specific grade-by-grade knowledge students are expected to learn.
all_claims.source_passage: Accountability hawks need to show more grit in creating state and local curriculum standards. They should join with parents and teachers to help define the grade-by-grade knowledge that all the children in their local purview should gain.

all_claims.text: Teachers face professional risks, including job security, if they do not comply with instructions to prioritize test preparation over content instruction.
all_claims.source_passage: The comments by teachers on my piece were sobering. They carried this message: “Well, Mr. Hirsch, that’s all very well, but if you were in my shoes you’d realize that your job would be at stake if you did not do test preparation as instructed.”

all_claims.text: Teacher quality is not an innate characteristic or an inherent competence.
all_claims.source_passage: The effectiveness of a teacher is not some inherent competence, as the phrase teacher quality suggests.

all_claims.text: Statistical analysis of teacher effectiveness is misleading unless it is supported by a valid theory of underlying causes.
all_claims.source_passage: Statistical analysis is indispensable, but can be very misleading unless supported by a valid theory of the underlying causes of the results.

all_claims.text: In a coherent school environment, most teachers can become highly effective.
all_claims.source_passage: I have witnessed over and over that in a coherent school most teachers can become highly effective.

all_claims.text: The sudden national focus on 'teacher quality' is a result of reform fatigue and desperation following the failure of previous reforms.
all_claims.source_passage: I think it may be reform fatigue, possibly desperation. We are blaming teachers because of our disappointments with the results of our reforms.

all_claims.text: The state standards movement and No Child Left Behind (NCLB) have failed to significantly improve outcomes for high school students.
all_claims.source_passage: The state standards movement and the No Child Left Behind law have left high school students just about as far behind as they were before the law was instituted.

all_claims.text: Charter schools' overall results are not significantly better than those of regular public schools.
all_claims.source_passage: Charter schools, despite their laudable triumphs, are highly uneven in quality. Their overall results are not much better than those of regular schools.

all_claims.text: Educational reforms have failed because they have been primarily structural rather than focused on grade-by-grade curricular coherence.
all_claims.source_passage: But the most likely cause of disappointing results from the various reforms is that they have been primarily structural in character. They have not systematically grappled with the grade-by-grade specifics and coherence of the elementary school curriculum.

all_claims.text: The theory that reading consists of content-independent skills is mistaken and has allowed reformers to evade the issue of specific grade-by-grade content.
all_claims.source_passage: The underlying theory of the reforms (reflected in state reading standards) has been that schools are teaching skills that can be developed by any suitable content. That mistaken theory has allowed the problem of grade-by-grade content to be evaded.

all_claims.text: Current standardized reading tests are invalid because they do not test the specific content being taught in the classroom.
all_claims.source_passage: They apply the notions received in their training, and do what they are told to do by their administrators, under the ever-present threat of reading tests that do not actually test the content that is being taught.

all_claims.text: Teacher unions focus on contract stipulations and 'bread-and-butter' concessions because the classroom environment has become unproductive and punitive.
all_claims.source_passage: Under these extremely unfavorable conditions of work, it’s no wonder that teacher unions have focused on bread-and-butter concessions—and have pushed back against punitive but unproductive reforms.

all_claims.text: United States teacher preparation programs have failed to adequately prepare teachers for classroom management or subject-matter instruction for over fifty years.
all_claims.source_passage: It’s true that in the United States, there has been a deep problem with teacher preparation for more than half a century. We have a system that, according to teachers themselves, does not prepare them adequately for classroom management or the substance of what they must teach.

all_claims.text: School systems can decline even with highly qualified teachers if a substantive curriculum is replaced by fallacious skill-based ideas.
all_claims.source_passage: Yet, as I will illustrate with the example of France, even with a staff of well-trained, highly qualified, and well-educated teachers, the schools can suddenly decline when a substantive curriculum is abandoned, and fallacious ideas about skills begin to dominate.

all_claims.text: Teacher 'quality,' when measured by student outcomes, improves when teachers are provided with a specific and coherent curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: If 'teacher quality' is to be judged by outcomes, their quality had suddenly risen [at Three Oaks School].

all_claims.text: An average teacher in a content-cumulative system is more effective than a superb teacher in a content-incoherent system.
all_claims.source_passage: Within the content-incoherent American primary school, it is impossible for a superb teacher to be as effective as a merely average teacher is in the content-cumulative Japanese elementary school.

all_claims.text: Successful teaching is more reliably produced by the coherence of the wider school system and teacher cooperation than by the individual brilliance of teachers.
all_claims.source_passage: The incident illustrated how good teaching can often depend more reliably on the coherence of the wider system, and the cooperation it brings, than on virtuoso performances.

all_claims.text: A specific and coherent curriculum allows the work of each teacher to build cumulatively on the work of their predecessors.
all_claims.source_passage: In a system with a specific and coherent curriculum, the work of each teacher builds on the work of teachers who came before.

all_claims.text: Curricular cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness provide a greater boost to student achievement than individual teacher brilliance in an incoherent system.
all_claims.source_passage: The three Cs—cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness—yield a bigger boost than the most brilliant efforts of teachers working individually against the odds within a topic-incoherent system.

all_claims.text: Systemic coherence improves teachers both individually and collectively.
all_claims.source_passage: A more coherent system makes teachers better individually and hugely better collectively.

all_claims.text: American teachers are victims of inadequate educational theories rather than being the source of intellectual failings.
all_claims.source_passage: American teachers (along with their students) are, in short, the tragic victims of inadequate theories. They are being blamed for intellectual failings that permeate the system within which they must work.

all_claims.text: The educational crisis in America is a problem of idea quality, not teacher quality.
all_claims.source_passage: The real problem is idea quality, not teacher quality.

all_claims.text: Teacher training has been compromised by theories of developmentalism, individualism, and the skills delusion.
all_claims.source_passage: The difficulty lies not with the inherent abilities of teachers but with the theories that have watered down their training, and created an intellectually chaotic school environment based on developmentalism, individualism, and the skills delusion.

all_claims.text: A specific curriculum would allow teachers to prepare more effectively and ensure they master necessary content.
all_claims.source_passage: Then teachers could prepare themselves more effectively, and teacher training could ensure that teacher candidates have mastered the content they will be responsible for teaching.

all_claims.text: Technological solutions like blended learning are based on a 'skills delusion' and have a history of failure in primary education.
all_claims.source_passage: Those who hope to find amelioration of the “teacher quality problem” through the use of computers and “blended learning” may be fostering yet another skills delusion. Technological fixes haven’t worked in the past.

all_claims.text: Primary education requires an empathetic personal connection that technology cannot provide.
all_claims.source_passage: Young students rely on an empathetic personal connection that not even our most advanced computer-adaptive programs can deliver.

all_claims.text: The common claim that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable for student learning is incorrect.
all_claims.source_passage: The assertion that “Teacher quality affects student learning more than any other school-based variable” is not footnoted. According to two summaries of research by Dr. Russ Whitehurst, a better curriculum can range from being slightly to dramatically more effective than a better teacher.

all_claims.text: Curriculum quality is more impactful than teacher quality because curriculum constitutes the actual content of learning.
all_claims.source_passage: That’s not surprising when you consider that the curriculum is what teachers teach and what students are supposed to learn.

all_claims.text: Value-Added Measures (VAMs) of teacher effectiveness are inherently unreliable and variable for language arts.
all_claims.source_passage: But, in fact, the results themselves cry out that something is amiss, since the value-added principle has exhibited far more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for math.

all_claims.text: Math tests are more accurate measures of teacher effectiveness than reading tests because math tests correlate highly with the taught curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: In math, as I showed in chapter 1, there is a high correlation between what is supposed to be taught and what is actually tested, whereas that’s not true for the language arts curriculum and current reading tests.

all_claims.text: The value-added principle exhibits significantly more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for mathematics.
all_claims.source_passage: the value-added principle has exhibited far more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for math.

all_claims.text: Current reading tests fail to accurately gauge the specific value a teacher has added to a student's reading comprehension over a year.
all_claims.source_passage: Our current reading tests cannot in fact reliably and validly gauge the value the teacher has added.

all_claims.text: Reading test scores are a reflection of knowledge and vocabulary acquired from all life sources, not just school.
all_claims.source_passage: Scores on reading tests reflect knowledge and vocabulary gained from all sources.

all_claims.text: Advantaged students consistently build academic knowledge both inside and outside of school, whereas disadvantaged students rely almost exclusively on school for such knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: Advantaged students are constantly building up academic knowledge from both inside and outside the school. Disadvantaged students gain their academic knowledge mainly inside school

all_claims.text: Current reading tests are curriculum-blind and therefore cannot measure how well a specific school curriculum has been taught.
all_claims.source_passage: Not being curriculum based, they cannot be an accurate means of testing how well the particular knowledge in the school curriculum has been imparted.

all_claims.text: The assumption that reading comprehension is a general, content-independent skill is a technical and theoretical error.
all_claims.source_passage: The implicit assumption that “general reading skill” is itself the content of the curriculum is a technical mistake and an incorrect assumption.

all_claims.text: Judges in legal proceedings should rule against the use of value-added measures for rating language arts teachers due to the falsity of their underlying assumptions.
all_claims.source_passage: Any judge in a lawsuit, properly alerted to the falsity of their assumptions, should rule against the fairness of value-added measures for rating language arts teachers.

all_claims.text: Test-based evaluations have caused the demoralization of millions of public school teachers.
all_claims.source_passage: Another evil consequence of test-based evaluations of language arts teachers has been the demoralization of millions of public school teachers.

all_claims.text: Teachers and administrators have been indoctrinated in individualistic, child-centered educational theories.
all_claims.source_passage: Like their administrators, they have been indoctrinated in individualistic, child-centered education.

all_claims.text: Teachers should reject the 'skills delusion' and demand a coherent, cumulative, multiyear content-based curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: My plea to teachers—for the sake of their students, and themselves—is to rebel against the skills delusion; to insist on coherent and cumulative multiyear content

all_claims.text: Schoolteachers should not receive tenure protection in cases where they fail to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.
all_claims.source_passage: But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”; nor should there be for schoolteachers.

all_claims.text: Principals should prioritize spending on teacher development and the creation of collaborative planning environments over other expenditures.
all_claims.source_passage: If I were a principal in a primary school I’d spend my money on teachers, on their ongoing development, and on creating conditions in which the work of teachers in one grade supports the work of teachers in the next

all_claims.text: Tenure protections at universities were originally instituted to prevent the censorship of opinion.
all_claims.source_passage: Tenure protections at universities were instituted to avoid censorship of opinion.

all_claims.text: University tenure does not provide protection for a faculty member's failure to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.
all_claims.source_passage: But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”

all_claims.text: Primary school funding should prioritize teacher development and the creation of conditions that facilitate grade-to-grade pedagogical support.
all_claims.source_passage: If I were a principal in a primary school I’d spend my money on teachers, on their ongoing development, and on creating conditions in which the work of teachers in one grade supports the work of teachers in the next

all_claims.text: Teachers require dedicated time for collaborative planning and consultation with their colleagues.
all_claims.source_passage: and in which teachers would have time to consult and collaboratively plan.

all_claims.text: Educational success over a twelve-year period is the result of slow, consistent progress rather than intermittent bursts of individual teacher brilliance.
all_claims.source_passage: Its success depends on slow but sure progress, not bursts of brilliance—welcome as those are when talented teachers inspire a whole class.

all_claims.text: Collaboratively compiled handbooks and shared pedagogical strategies are more effective for solving student learning difficulties than individual teacher intuition.
all_claims.source_passage: The teacher shook her head: no, it wasn’t her brilliance that produced the result, and from her desk drawer she took out a handbook that teachers had cooperatively compiled. “Here it is,” she said. “It’s suggested as a good tack to try when you run into that situation.”

all_claims.text: The focus on teacher quality as the primary lever for reform is a symptom of 'reform fatigue' and desperation after previous structural strategies failed.
all_claims.source_passage: Only in the last few years has the teacher quality issue risen to the top. I think it may be reform fatigue, possibly desperation. We are blaming teachers because of our disappointments with the results of our reforms.

all_claims.text: When classroom environments become a 'purgatory' due to punitive reforms, teachers and unions logically shift their focus toward contract stipulations and bread-and-butter concessions.
all_claims.source_passage: When the classroom, which should be a daily reward, becomes a purgatory, one turns to contract stipulations.

all_claims.text: American teachers face the structural disadvantage of having to manage large discrepancies in student academic preparation, a problem Japanese teachers do not face due to their coherent system.
all_claims.source_passage: For one thing, the American teacher has to deal with big discrepancies in student academic preparation, while the Japanese teacher does not.

all_claims.text: Computers and 'blended learning' solutions are likely to be another 'skills delusion' because technological fixes have consistently failed in the past.
all_claims.source_passage: Those who hope to find amelioration of the 'teacher quality problem' through the use of computers and 'blended learning' may be fostering yet another skills delusion. Technological fixes haven’t worked in the past.

all_claims.text: Computer programs are inherently limited because they cannot provide the empathetic personal connection that young students rely on for primary education.
all_claims.source_passage: Young students rely on an empathetic personal connection that not even our most advanced computer-adaptive programs can deliver.

all_claims.text: The common assertion that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable is a non-footnoted claim that is contradicted by research from Dr. Russ Whitehurst.
all_claims.source_passage: The assertion that 'Teacher quality affects student learning more than any other school-based variable' is not footnoted. According to two summaries of research by Dr. Russ Whitehurst, a better curriculum can range from being slightly to dramatically more effective than a better teacher.

all_claims.text: Reading tests are at best 70 percent accurate at the individual level, making them statistically insufficient for high-stakes individual teacher evaluations.
all_claims.source_passage: Reading tests at best are 70 percent accurate at the individual level. The inherent uncertainty of the school-based contribution to a student’s reading scores between one year and another must reduce the validity of test inferences even more.

all_claims.text: Teachers should actively 'rebel' against the skills delusion and insist on a multiyear content-based curriculum for their own professional well-being.
all_claims.source_passage: My plea to teachers—for the sake of their students, and themselves—is to rebel against the skills delusion; to insist on coherent and cumulative multiyear content; then cooperate and consult.

all_claims.text: The inability of computers to accurately translate language, even after decades of development, serves as a theoretical proof of their limitations in primary education.
all_claims.source_passage: For example, after decades of work and billions spent, computers cannot accurately translate from one language to another. Probably they can’t even in theory. Such current limitations do not lend confidence that they can transform primary education.

all_claims.text: Preschool academic benefits for disadvantaged children in the United States typically fade out after two years of primary school.
all_claims.source_passage: Federal reports going back several decades show that in the United States preschool academic benefits for disadvantaged children fade out after two years of primary school.

all_claims.text: After first or second grade, there is often no detectable difference in IQ, vocabulary, or achievement between disadvantaged children who attended Head Start and those who did not.
all_claims.source_passage: After first or second grade there is no detectable difference in IQ, vocabulary size, or academic achievement between disadvantaged children who attended Head Start and disadvantaged children who did not.

all_claims.text: Preschool fadeout is not inevitable, as preschool in other nations and specific circumstances has shown permanently positive effects.
all_claims.source_passage: We know that it did not inevitably disappear, because preschool in other nations and other circumstances has a permanently positive effect.

all_claims.text: The appearance of preschool fadeout in France after 1990 was caused by changes in elementary school curriculum, not changes in the preschools themselves.
all_claims.source_passage: The excellent preschools had not changed; only the elementary schools had. That is a crucial piece of evidence in the preschool fadeout puzzle.

all_claims.text: A second form of fadeout occurs between middle school and high school, where verbal gains made by thirteen-year-olds disappear by age seventeen.
all_claims.source_passage: The progress they made as thirteen-year-olds has disappeared. So there is fadeout after middle school as well as after preschool.

all_claims.text: There is an 'equity fadeout' in the United States where achievement gaps widen significantly between age thirteen and age seventeen.
all_claims.source_passage: Moreover, there’s another form of late fadeout: the achievement gap between groups at age thirteen has widened at age seventeen. There is an equity fadeout too, with gaps widening after middle school.

all_claims.text: All versions of fadeout can be attributed to the incoherence of an elementary curriculum that fails to build on preschool gains or lay groundwork for future success.
all_claims.source_passage: We now have good evidence that all of these versions of fadeout can be blamed on the incoherence of an elementary curriculum that fails to build upon the gains of preschool and also fails to lay further groundwork for future success.

all_claims.text: Reading test scores in elementary school fail to capture the true extent of a child's verbal progress, knowledge, and vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: Children’s scores on a reading test in elementary school don’t capture all of the verbal progress children may or may not have made in the primary grades. Test makers deliberately limit the range of knowledge and vocabulary tested.

all_claims.text: Students' reading and writing abilities lag behind their speaking and listening abilities until approximately grade 7.
all_claims.source_passage: Students’ reading and writing abilities lag behind actual speaking and listening abilities until about grade 7.

all_claims.text: There is a latency period in word acquisition where partial knowledge is formed but not yet reflected in standard test scores.
all_claims.source_passage: Recent work on vocabulary growth in relation to test scores has shown what has always been inferred by specialists: there is a latency period in a child’s knowledge of a word.

all_claims.text: The elementary curriculum is the primary cause of the fadeout problem at all ages.
all_claims.source_passage: A thesis of this chapter, along with important policy implications, is that while good preschools and high schools are extremely important, the practical nub of the fadeout problem at all ages is the elementary curriculum.

all_claims.text: Public investment in preschool only makes sense if it is followed by a primary school that consolidates and extends the early boost.
all_claims.source_passage: It does make sense—but only if a good preschool is followed by a good primary school that consolidates and extends the early boost.

all_claims.text: The foundation for future verbal growth in grades K–4 is not readily apparent from test scores during those early grades because it exists as latent knowledge and incipient vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: This means that the foundation for future verbal growth by students in grades K–4 is not readily apparent from test scores during those early grades. But the soil is tilled, and the seeds are planted for later growth; they just aren’t yet visible.

all_claims.text: Latent knowledge gained in early elementary grades becomes potent and critical in the years following grade 4, even if it is invisible to tests at ages nine and thirteen.
all_claims.source_passage: Latent knowledge may be invisible to tests at ages nine and thirteen, but it is critical—and becomes potent—in the years that follow.

all_claims.text: The substance of what is learned in grades K–5 determines whether disadvantaged students will catch up and whether students will be ready for high school and college.
all_claims.source_passage: The substance of what is learned in grades K–5 will determine whether disadvantaged students will start catching up to their peers, whether middle school students will be ready for high school, and whether high school students will be ready for colleges or careers.

all_claims.text: All three forms of fadeout can be forestalled by following a communal-knowledge-based preschool with a communal-knowledge-based elementary school.
all_claims.source_passage: A communal-knowledge-based preschool should be followed by a communal-knowledge-based elementary school. All three forms of fadeout could then be forestalled, as this chapter will show.

all_claims.text: Direct Instruction and Success for All are the most fully documented and effective early reading programs in the United States for overcoming disadvantage.
all_claims.source_passage: Among programs designed to overcome disadvantage in the early grades, two stand out as the most fully studied and successful: Direct Instruction and Success for All (SFA). . . No educational programs in the United States have likely been more fully documented as being effective early reading programs.

all_claims.text: Scripted programs like Direct Instruction and Success for All succeeded better than naturalistic approaches in teaching children to sound out printed text.
all_claims.source_passage: They both succeeded better than more naturalistic approaches in bringing children to elementary competence in sounding out printed text.

all_claims.text: Natural-growth and Reggio Emilia–style preschools had the least effect on disadvantaged students in Project Follow Through.
all_claims.source_passage: Developmental, natural-growth, Reggio Emilia–style preschools had the least effect; deliberate, step-by-step skill instruction had the greater positive effect on both attainment and on the attitude of the young students.

all_claims.text: American schools largely ignored the positive results of Direct Instruction because step-by-step skill instruction was perceived as developmentally inappropriate.
all_claims.source_passage: Few schools paid attention to these results, however, since step-by-step skill instruction was felt to be childhood-killing and developmentally inappropriate—an early illustration of the invincibility of developmental ideas about early childhood.

all_claims.text: The intensive-skills approach of Direct Instruction fails to sustain comprehension gains past grade 3.
all_claims.source_passage: But now it became clear that the intensive-skills approach of Direct Instruction had not succeeded either. Project Follow Through evaluations had stopped at grade 3. What happened to Direct Instruction students in grades 5 and 6? . . . they could not understand what the texts were saying any better than students who had not had Direct Instruction—a null effect in the end.

all_claims.text: Success for All does not avoid fadeout when scaled up to real-world evaluations.
all_claims.source_passage: When scaled up so that credible real-world evaluations can now be made, it has been found that Success for All does not avoid fadeout.

all_claims.text: Natural-development programs and intensive-skills programs are similar in that they both offer a fragmented curriculum from the standpoint of knowledge building.
