all_claims.source_passage: The only enduring way to raise achievement and narrow gaps between groups of students is by closing the knowledge and vocabulary gap.

all_claims.text: The most egalitarian school model is one that utilizes a cumulative, multiyear plan for building knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: The most egalitarian school is one that follows a cumulative, multiyear plan of knowledge building.

all_claims.text: Knowledge is the ultimate underlying basis of standardized test scores.
all_claims.source_passage: Over time, this inevitably happens when a knowledge sequence is followed faithfully, because the ultimate basis of test scores is knowledge.

all_claims.text: If a school narrows the knowledge gap between rich and poor students, the gap in their test scores will also narrow.
all_claims.source_passage: If the word/knowledge gap is narrowed, so will be the gap in test scores between rich and poor.

all_claims.text: Any primary school, regardless of student demographics, can achieve reading scores equal to affluent areas if they implement a knowledge-based curriculum over several years.
all_claims.source_passage: If PS 124 in Queens, New York, composed of immigrant, poor, and racially diverse students, can consistently post scores that are equal to reading scores in more affluent zip codes, then any primary school can do the same—over a period of years.

all_claims.text: Impoverished students require more background knowledge rather than more skill-based drills to succeed.
all_claims.source_passage: these two brave women decided that what their students needed was not more skill drills but more knowledge. The students in Queens, they concluded, needed to learn the same things that children in Scarsdale knew.

all_claims.text: A content-rich curriculum increases student engagement and teacher collaboration.
all_claims.source_passage: Using the new curriculum as a foundation, we increased teacher collaboration and student engagement.

all_claims.text: A knowledge-based curriculum can significantly reduce student and teacher mobility within a school.
all_claims.source_passage: With a homeless shelter two blocks away, student mobility used to be high, but after Core Knowledge was introduced in 1999–2000, parents went to great lengths to keep their children in PS/MS 124... Ever since we adopted Core Knowledge, teacher mobility also dropped—and remains low to this day.

all_claims.text: High-level content is not 'too rigorous' for students because historical events and scientific discoveries are inherently interesting.
all_claims.source_passage: At first we feared that the content would be too rigorous... We soon discovered that all of us loved being challenged. Major historical events, scientific discoveries, and works of art are inherently interesting; with our new curriculum, teachers and children loved learning.

all_claims.text: Adopting a Core Knowledge curriculum can lead to a significant drop in teacher mobility.
all_claims.source_passage: Ever since we adopted Core Knowledge, teacher mobility also dropped—and remains low to this day.

all_claims.text: Rigorous academic content in history, science, and art is inherently interesting to both educators and students.
all_claims.source_passage: Major historical events, scientific discoveries, and works of art are inherently interesting; with our new curriculum, teachers and children loved learning.

all_claims.text: The background knowledge gained through a content-rich curriculum in elementary grades is sustained and extended into middle school.
all_claims.source_passage: The results indicated that the background knowledge they gleaned through Core Knowledge in the elementary grades was sustained and extended.

all_claims.text: Standard state and district instructional mandates often fail to inspire students to learn because they prioritize rote approaches over deep knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: It became apparent that while the teachers were planning well and adhering to state and district instructional mandates, the students were not being inspired to learn.

all_claims.text: A shared knowledge sequence allows for powerful 'embedded professional development' where teachers support each other's content mastery.
all_claims.source_passage: By sharing their instructional plans with each other, they expanded each other’s knowledge base in all subjects. This embedded professional development was powerful.

all_claims.text: Content-rich schooling prepares students for citizenship, college, and diverse careers better than skills-based schooling.
all_claims.source_passage: Many expressed, “This is why I became a teacher—to provide students with instruction that prepares them for citizenship, college, and all sorts of careers.”

all_claims.text: A knowledge-rich curriculum awakens a student's innate desire to learn and fosters spontaneous academic engagement.
all_claims.source_passage: We were finally awakening their desire to learn. Very early on, students began asking more questions and even discussing the content among themselves. Academic debates happened on the playground.

all_claims.text: Inner-city primary schools often lack a spirit of community and common purpose due to a focus on individualized skills curricula.
all_claims.source_passage: The usual atmosphere of our inner-city primary schools is far from happy or cohesive among either teachers or students. A spirit of community built upon a common purpose is often lacking.

all_claims.text: Emphasizing individuality in early education is a paradox that thwarts actual individuality by failing to deliver shared knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: There’s an overlooked paradox in emphasizing individuality in early years; it defeats itself. It fails to deliver the shared knowledge of the community. That cramps children’s competence and thus their freedom to develop their individual tastes and abilities. Individualism in the primary curriculum thwarts individuality.

all_claims.text: True individuality develops only through and in relation to the social community and its shared norms.
all_claims.source_passage: It’s in relation to the community that individuality develops. Only by mastering the shared knowledge and norms of the community can one diverge from those norms effectively.

all_claims.text: The basis of language is commonality, and individual style is built upon mastery of shared conventions.
all_claims.source_passage: The basis of language is commonality. Individuality comes from mastery of its shared conventions; that mastery is the foundation of an individual style.

all_claims.text: Ignorance of group conventions results in ineptitude and inequality rather than fostering individuality.
all_claims.source_passage: Ignorance of group conventions doesn’t foster individuality but ineptitude and inequality.

all_claims.text: Common subject matter creates a sense of common purpose that reduces alienation and misbehavior in schools.
all_claims.source_passage: Common subject matter sponsors a sense of common purpose in which students and teachers feel they are a part. Core Knowledge schools are happy schools. There is no sense of the alienation so pervasive in our public schools, and so productive of misbehavior.

all_claims.text: Schools that faithfully implement Core Knowledge principles raise achievement and narrow achievement gaps more effectively than comparison schools.
all_claims.source_passage: Studies have uniformly shown that schools that faithfully implement Core Knowledge–style principles have raised achievement and narrowed gaps far more effectively than comparison schools.

all_claims.text: The success of Core Knowledge schools validates the principle of a specific, communal, grade-by-grade curriculum rather than just a specific program.
all_claims.source_passage: Their widespread success is best viewed not as evidence for a program but for a principle—that of a specific, communal, grade-by-grade curriculum. I call the general principle 'lowercase core knowledge.'

all_claims.text: The curricular principle of specific grade-by-grade knowledge is the best-grounded and most successful in the world.
all_claims.source_passage: Educational research over the globe has already established that its curricular principle is the best grounded and most successful in the world.

all_claims.text: Core Knowledge is not a scripted program, and its specific contents are adaptable by schools.
all_claims.source_passage: The precise contents of the Core Knowledge Sequence are not the Tables of the Law. Core Knowledge is not a scripted program. Schools adjust the specifics.

all_claims.text: Individualized skills-based approaches current in American schools yield poor results and lack scientific grounding.
all_claims.source_passage: Those principles are far better grounded in science and in practical results than are the individualized skills approaches with uncertain subject matters current in American schools, which continue to yield poor results.

all_claims.text: National school systems that specify content and expertise levels for each grade level achieve the best results in achievement and equity.
all_claims.source_passage: PISA analysts have stated explicitly that the best results in achievement and equity are found in national school systems in which all parties—parents, teachers, and students—are made aware of the specific content and level of expertise that each student is expected to attain at each grade level.

all_claims.text: A systematic approach to knowledge building is more productive than an arbitrary skills approach.
all_claims.source_passage: Both PISA and common sense affirm that a systematic approach to knowledge building is more productive than an arbitrary skills approach with unspecified topics.

all_claims.text: The American educational research community has failed to conduct large-scale longitudinal studies of Core Knowledge despite its longevity and uniqueness.
all_claims.source_passage: Yet scientific curiosity has not induced the much-needed large study.

all_claims.text: Educational specialists in the United States should not state that Core Knowledge is an 'unproved' program when they have actively declined to fund or conduct the large-scale studies necessary to prove it.
all_claims.source_passage: In sum, educational specialists in the United States (those who accept the three disabling ideas traced in the prologue) should not continue to state that Core Knowledge is an 'unproved' program, on the grounds that they have declined to prove it.

all_claims.text: Core Knowledge is not merely a specific program but a curricular principle supported by an immense body of international evidence and research.
all_claims.source_passage: It is not a program but a curricular principle for which there is an immense body of international evidence and research.

all_claims.text: The small-scale studies of Core Knowledge in the US yield positive results that confirm the large-scale findings of international PISA reports.
all_claims.source_passage: The small-scale studies of Core Knowledge, all positive, simply confirm the large-scale conclusions of the PISA reports.

all_claims.text: Successful PISA nations utilize curricular sequences that share six key characteristics: they are field-tested, topic-specific, well-rounded, coherent, cumulative, and selective.
all_claims.source_passage: The curricular sequences of the most successful PISA nations share six key characteristics with Core Knowledge.

all_claims.text: Topic specificity is the most important curricular characteristic to advocate for in the United States because it ensures all stakeholders know the required level of expertise at each stage.
all_claims.source_passage: Topic specificity is probably the most important characteristic to insist upon in the United States, since it makes everyone—teachers, students, and parents—aware of the level of expertise that students will have gained at each point.

all_claims.text: Breadth of knowledge and vocabulary, including proper nouns, is critical for high reading skill and communicative proficiency.
all_claims.source_passage: Such well roundedness is important also in a purely practical view, since breadth of knowledge and vocabulary (including proper nouns!) is critical to high reading skill and other communicative skills.

all_claims.text: Broad knowledge is a critical tool for ameliorating class distinctions and achieving social equality.
all_claims.source_passage: Broad knowledge is also critical for ameliorating class distinctions, and placing all citizens on a more equal footing.

all_claims.text: The individualized and disconnected nature of current American language arts topics hinders substantive learning and vocabulary acquisition.
all_claims.source_passage: Topic coherence within a grade level is particularly important in the language arts class, where topics in the United States are currently individualized, fragmentary, and disconnected. Such incoherence hinders substantive learning and vocabulary acquisition.

all_claims.text: To be effective for word acquisition, instruction must focus on sustained topics within the same 'word fields' for two weeks or more.
all_claims.source_passage: Sustained topics requiring the same 'word fields' need to last two weeks or more to be effective.

all_claims.text: A multiyear cumulative sequence of topics is technically necessary to ensure all students are prepared for upcoming lessons and no student is denied critical knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: Designing a multiyear cumulative sequence of topics is important technically to make sure that students are ready for each subject over the sequence of grade levels, assuring commonality across all classes within a grade level. No student is denied critical knowledge.

all_claims.text: The educational theory that any topic can be used to teach general skills is a fundamental empirical mistake that leads to failure.
all_claims.source_passage: The idea that any sensible topic will serve to inculcate general skills has been a fundamental empirical mistake that guarantees failure.

all_claims.text: Certain curricular topics, like Romeo and Juliet, are essential to social equality because ignorance of them leads to social exclusion and scorn.
all_claims.source_passage: Still others, like Romeo and Juliet, are essential to equality, because to be ignorant of them is to be ignorant of what others know, and thus to be scorned and excluded from comprehension and from the esteem of others.

all_claims.text: Shared knowledge in the elementary curriculum is the primary 'nourisher' of the public sphere.
all_claims.source_passage: They enhance a sense of community because everyone, not just the favored few, possesses the shared knowledge of the elementary curriculum: the great nourisher of the public sphere.

all_claims.text: The Washington School in Rochester, Minnesota, has posted the best test scores in the city and narrowed the achievement gap better than any other local school.
all_claims.source_passage: The school, the Washington School, was to be featured because it had been posting the best scores in the city, and also narrowed the achievement gap between advantaged and disadvantaged pupils better than any other local school.

all_claims.text: There is a massive unfilled popular demand for Core Knowledge schooling in Rochester, evidenced by a waiting list that is nearly three times the school's total capacity.
all_claims.source_passage: With a total capacity of 351 students in grades K–5, the school had a waiting list of over 1,000.

all_claims.text: It is currently extremely difficult to change basic educational ideas in the United States, even when there is significant parental demand for content-based curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: This is a pretty good example of how difficult it currently is to change basic educational ideas in the United States, even with a sizeable number of parents eager to see a shift toward a more enabling, content-based curriculum.

all_claims.text: Effective educational changes typically originate from school leadership and teachers rather than from parents.
all_claims.source_passage: The effective changes in ideas that I have seen over the years have come from within—from brave principals like Valarie Lewis and adventurous teachers—more than from parents

all_claims.text: Parents feel helpless to influence school curriculum because they lack confidence when faced with the sophisticated slogans of skill-centric doctrine.
all_claims.source_passage: parents, who feel helpless and unsure in the face of the highly definite viewpoints and brilliant slogans of skill-centric doctrine.

all_claims.text: A school that produces large yearly gains for all students is inherently gap-closing in its social effects.
all_claims.source_passage: We’ve known since the Coleman Report that a school that induces large yearly gains for all students is a school that is inherently gap-closing in its effects

all_claims.text: The American education system is paralyzed and generally only changes in unproductive directions under the threat of standardized testing.
all_claims.source_passage: From afar, the American system seems paralyzed, unable to change except under threats of tests, and then in unproductive, test-prep directions.

all_claims.text: Parents and educators are prevented from trying knowledge-based curricula because those approaches contradict the child-centered, skill-centric doctrines that hold an intellectual monopoly.
all_claims.source_passage: my settled belief, is that parents and educators in low-scoring elementary schools... are prevented from trying out a knowledge-based, communal curriculum because such an approach contradicts the child-centered, skill-centric doctrines that are still an intellectual monopoly in our education world.

all_claims.text: Adopting a knowledge-based curriculum is hindered by psychological barriers, including resistance to changing habits and anxiety about teaching unfamiliar subject matter.
all_claims.source_passage: To this psychological barrier must be added two others: an all-too-human resistance to changing one’s habitual mode of activity, and an anxiety about teaching unfamiliar subject matters.

all_claims.text: The internal difficulties of school reform can be overcome if a new belief system is established within the school under strong leadership.
all_claims.source_passage: But we know from decades of experience that these inherent difficulties can be overcome if a new belief system arises within the school under strong leadership.

all_claims.text: Seeing direct evidence of success, such as in the Washington School or PS 124, provides educators with a necessary sense of efficacy to pursue reform.
all_claims.source_passage: When people see the evidence of success with their own eyes, as in PS 124 and the Washington School, they gain a sense of efficacy.

all_claims.text: A district-wide curriculum is necessary to allow for cooperation and shared support across schools.
all_claims.source_passage: A district-wide curriculum should be the aim, because the entire district can then cooperate in giving schools the support they need.

all_claims.text: Directly witnessing the success of knowledge-based schooling in specific institutions builds a sense of efficacy among educators.
all_claims.source_passage: When people see the evidence of success with their own eyes, as in PS 124 and the Washington School, they gain a sense of efficacy.

all_claims.text: District-wide curriculum implementation is superior to school-by-school implementation because it enables district-level cooperation and support.
all_claims.source_passage: A district-wide curriculum should be the aim, because the entire district can then cooperate in giving schools the support they need in putting a challenging elementary curriculum into effect.

all_claims.text: In many American cities, the student mobility rate is approximately 30 percent, often occurring within the same district.
all_claims.source_passage: In most cities, the mobility rate is around 30 percent, sometimes higher, with grave educational consequences.

all_claims.text: The negative educational effects of high student mobility are exacerbated by discontinuities in subject matter between different schools.
all_claims.source_passage: The ill effects of mobility would be much alleviated if students did not have to suffer discontinuities in the subject matters they were studying.

all_claims.text: A district-wide curriculum provides educational equity for mobile, disadvantaged students by ensuring curricular continuity.
all_claims.source_passage: A further highly important value of districtwide implementation is rarely mentioned: the movement of disadvantaged students from school to school in the middle of the year for economic reasons... The ill effects of mobility would be much alleviated if students did not have to suffer discontinuities in the subject matters they were studying.

all_claims.text: Standardized reading comprehension tests are primarily measures of communal knowledge and vocabulary.
all_claims.source_passage: A reading comprehension test is a test of communal knowledge and vocabulary.

all_claims.text: Test-preparation schooling has demonstrated limited utility in significantly raising reading scores over the past decade.
all_claims.source_passage: After more than a decade of test-prep schooling we know that test-prep has limited utility in raising reading scores.

all_claims.text: A well-delivered communal elementary curriculum is the necessary prerequisite for all children to succeed on reading comprehension tests.
all_claims.source_passage: To enable all children to possess that knowledge requires a well-delivered, communal elementary curriculum that delivers such knowledge to all. If all children master such a curriculum, they will be enabled to score well on tests of reading comprehension. QED

all_claims.text: Instituting a specific districtwide curriculum is the most reliable way to ensure students master the communal knowledge required for success.
all_claims.source_passage: the surest and best way to ensure that children in a district master a communal curriculum is for the district to institute a specific districtwide curriculum.

all_claims.text: Districts should implement a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading tests while transitioning to a knowledge-rich curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: an imaginative superintendent could arrange to get a “research exemption” and call a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading comprehension tests.

all_claims.text: Locally-made content tests based on the school curriculum should be used as a more valid alternative to skills-based tests during curriculum reform.
all_claims.source_passage: The district could then substitute locally made content tests based on the school curriculum—in full confidence that after the moratorium, students would perform far better than before on the standard reading comprehension tests.

all_claims.text: Implementing a knowledge-based curriculum leads to a significant improvement in student and teacher morale.
all_claims.source_passage: A great improvement in the morale of students and teachers ensues, along with a great improvement in achievement and equity.

all_claims.text: Curriculum-based tests possess 'face validity' and are inherently fairer than skills-based tests because they test what was actually taught.
all_claims.source_passage: Curriculum-based tests are inherently fair. They possess “face validity.”

all_claims.text: The psychological resistance of Americans to imposed uniformity necessitates the creation of multiple alternative communal curricula rather than a single national mandate.
all_claims.source_passage: For widespread progress to be made, alternative communal curriculums will need to be created, since Americans do not like the idea of imposed uniformity. They demand a choice.

all_claims.text: Adopting a communal early curriculum is not a novel concept but a return to historical educational foundations.
all_claims.source_passage: My argument for a communal early curriculum is not, then, a novelty, nor is it an elaborate promotion of the Core Knowledge plan as being the unique way to sustain the common school in the United States.

all_claims.text: The common elementary curriculum in Japan and Finland is the reason parents in those nations see no functional need for charter schools.
all_claims.source_passage: That’s why parents in Finland and Japan have seen no reason to institute charter schools.

all_claims.text: The educational benefits of a 'head start' are temporary and will fade unless they are supported by a 'sustained start' through the primary grades.
all_claims.source_passage: Momentum is gained not just by a head start but by a sustained start. Lacking continued momentum, a mere head start slows down and fades out.

all_claims.text: According to the Hart-Risley research, a child from a professional family hears 45 million words by age four, compared to 26 million for a working-class child and 13 million for a welfare child.
all_claims.source_passage: During four years of observations, a child from a professional family would hear 45 million words, a working-class child 26 million, and a welfare child 13 million.

all_claims.text: Early education must be liberated from the 'exclusive trammels of the written word' to prevent the Matthew Effect from intensifying.
all_claims.source_passage: With it has come the realization that the education of young children needs also to be freed from the exclusive trammels of the written word.

all_claims.text: While isolated word study is moderately effective, its impact is too small relative to the significant amount of school time it consumes.
all_claims.source_passage: Isolated word study, well conducted, is moderately effective but has not significantly narrowed gaps between haves and have-nots. It takes too much time for too small an effect.

all_claims.text: Antonio Gramsci argued that the common school should be a 'school of humanistic formation' or a 'school of general culture.'
all_claims.source_passage: That was the reason Gramsci insisted that the unitary “common school” should be a “school of humanistic formation or a school of general culture.”

all_claims.text: District superintendents could facilitate curriculum reform by securing a 'research exemption' and a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading tests.
all_claims.source_passage: For instance, while the changeover is under way, an imaginative superintendent could arrange to get a “research exemption” and call a three-year moratorium on skills-based reading comprehension tests.

all_claims.text: The inclusion of specific cultural figures like Rosa Parks in the curriculum is a result of deliberate activism by cultural activists.
all_claims.source_passage: The addition of Rosa Parks to canonical knowledge has been the deliberate work of cultural activists, and more power to them.

all_claims.text: A content-based curriculum is 'necessarily traditional' due to the inescapable inertia of knowledge shared by hundreds of millions of people.
all_claims.source_passage: Because of an inherent and inescapable inertia in the knowledge that is shared among hundreds of millions of people, the Core Knowledge plan was necessarily traditional.

all_claims.text: In the 1940s, American K–12 education ranked among the best and fairest school systems in the world.
all_claims.source_passage: In the 1940s, American K–12 education still ranked among the best and fairest school systems in the world.

all_claims.text: American K-12 education currently ranks below average in both achievement and equity.
all_claims.source_passage: Now it ranks below average in both achievement and equity.

all_claims.text: The American educational decline was caused by the adoption of an early-nineteenth-century Middle European educational ideology (progressive education).
all_claims.source_passage: Our educational decline began when we adopted an early-nineteenth-century Middle European educational ideology whose ideas took root in America beginning in the 1870s.

all_claims.text: Asian nations resisted progressive educational philosophy and subsequently achieved the best elementary school systems in the world.
all_claims.source_passage: Asian nations resisted the philosophy. They have made the fastest progress in the twentieth century, and can now boast the best elementary school systems in the world.

all_claims.text: The 'Great American Test Score Decline' between 1960 and 1980 was causally connected to the dominance of romantic, progressive ideas in schools.
all_claims.source_passage: The Great American Test Score Decline took place between 1960 and 1980. Scores have never come back. This book has explained the causal connections between the gradual dominance of romantic ideas and our educational decline.

all_claims.text: Educational doctrines favoring natural development and hands-on projects over rote learning have precluded the implementation of a well-rounded elementary curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: These doctrines have precluded the thing most needed in early education—a definite, well-rounded, elementary curriculum mastered by all students except those who are severely impaired.

all_claims.text: The current American resistance to a local common curriculum is a 20th-century 'post-romantic' tradition, not an original American tradition.
all_claims.source_passage: Current American resistance to the idea of a local common curriculum is not, then, a reflection of American traditions. It is a post-romantic tradition of the twentieth century.

all_claims.text: Effective educational reforms can only take hold once the unsuccessful ideology of 'providential individualism' is dethroned.
all_claims.source_passage: Once that outmoded and unsuccessful ideology is dethroned, effective educational reforms can take hold, but not before.

all_claims.text: Thinking skills such as 'critical thinking' and 'problem solving' are not productive educational aims.
all_claims.source_passage: We can start breaking free from that intellectual monopoly when the wider public understands that thinking skills—like “critical and creative thinking” and “problem solving”—are not productive educational aims.

all_claims.text: There is no such thing as developing a general, content-independent skill.
all_claims.source_passage: As Anders Ericsson and Robert Poole put it bluntly in their authoritative 2016 book on the subject: “There is no such thing as developing a general skill.”

all_claims.text: Language competence is dependent on subject-matter mastery built through a grade-by-grade curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: Language competence will then be correctly understood as dependent on subject-matter mastery, gradually built up in a well-rounded school under a specific, grade-by-grade curriculum.

all_claims.text: The most important academic attention schools can give an individual child is ensuring their mastery of a communal curriculum leading to Standard American English.
all_claims.source_passage: The most important academic attention that can be paid to the individual child in the elementary grades is to ensure his or her mastery of a communal curriculum that leads to mastery of Standard American English.

all_claims.text: Shared conventions of knowledge and sentiment are intrinsic components of language mastery.
all_claims.source_passage: We now know that such shared conventions of knowledge and sentiment are part and parcel of language mastery.

all_claims.text: Multiculturalism supports social justice only if it maintains the acquisition of larger, central communal expertise as a primary educational aim.
all_claims.source_passage: Multiculturalism helps social justice only if it keeps that larger, central expertise as an aim.

all_claims.text: Excluding students from central shared knowledge renders them incompetent communicators and maintains their poverty.
all_claims.source_passage: To exclude students from that central knowledge is to keep them incompetent communicators—and poor.

all_claims.text: The majority of shared knowledge required for expertise in print, media, commerce, and the internet changes slowly.
all_claims.source_passage: But most of the shared knowledge that makes a student expert in the language of print and TV, and commerce and the Internet, is knowledge at the center that changes slowly.

all_claims.text: The success of the American democratic experiment depends on preserving national unity through shared principles and education.
all_claims.source_passage: The success or failure of the experiment depended on preserving national unity as a far-flung community dedicated to common principles.

all_claims.text: A democratic nation requires a shared commitment to ideals that must be explicitly taught in schools and other public institutions.
all_claims.source_passage: Lincoln back in 1838 invoked the idea that the new kind of democratic nation required shared commitment to ideals gained through education: ‘Let it be taught in schools, in seminaries, and in colleges; let it be written in Primers, spelling books, and in Almanacs’

all_claims.text: America is intended to be a 'trans-tribal tribe' that overcomes divisive human tribal instincts through shared national identity.
all_claims.source_passage: America was to be a trans-tribal tribe. That paradoxical and noble aim has been an attempt to overcome the deep, divisive tribal instinct, the source of so many human ills.

all_claims.text: Jean Piaget's later revision of his theory of mental development constitutes a repudiation of the romantic principle of naturalistic education.
all_claims.source_passage: That statement is nothing less than a repudiation of the basic romantic principle of education; Piaget conceded that ‘training’ is, after all, just as potent as ‘growth’ in human development.

all_claims.text: The stages of mental growth are powerfully influenced by the specific content of education rather than being a purely natural developmental process.
all_claims.source_passage: He concluded that the stages of mental growth did not reflect a purely developmental process but were powerfully influenced by the specific content of education.

all_claims.text: Specific educational training can accelerate or delay the developmental process by as much as four years.
all_claims.source_passage: He concluded that the developmental process could be speeded or delayed by as much as four years. ‘Maturation,’ he said, ‘is not everything.’

all_claims.text: American educational thought remains dominated by romantic ideas that are inconsistent with current scientific evidence.
all_claims.source_passage: Exactly such a thought revolution, and a willingness to follow the evidence where it leads, is needed far and wide in educational thought in the United States, still dominated by romantic ideas.

all_claims.text: The growth of expertise in language and other domains is a slow, domain-restricted process with no significant shortcuts.
all_claims.source_passage: We have learned that the growth of expertise in language and other areas is a slow, domain-restricted growth. There are no significant shortcuts to intellectual competence.

all_claims.text: Domain-specific knowledge and long practice are essential requirements for consolidating any skill in long-term memory.
all_claims.source_passage: Domain-specific knowledge and long practice are essential to consolidating a skill in long-term memory.

all_claims.text: Neither computers nor general critical-thinking techniques can bypass the need for domain-specific knowledge in achieving intellectual competence.
all_claims.source_passage: Neither computers nor general critical-thinking techniques can circumvent those arduous requirements.

all_claims.text: Causal insights from basic science are a more reliable guide to educational policy than classroom-level research with uncontrolled variables.
all_claims.source_passage: Causal insight from basic science is truly generalizable, and is thus a far more reliable guide to policy.

all_claims.text: Large-scale epidemiological studies of nations are more reliable for educational research than classroom-level studies.
all_claims.source_passage: Also large-scale epidemiological studies of whole nations and millions of students are more reliable than classroom-level studies.

all_claims.text: The most persuasive indication that a finding is correct is intellectual triangulation, where the same result is derived from multiple independent approaches.
all_claims.source_passage: one of the most persuasive indications that a finding is correct is that the same result can be derived from multiple separate approaches that are completely independent of one another. Such intellectual triangulation has now occurred in cognitive science with respect to education.

all_claims.text: There are no significant shortcuts to achieving intellectual competence.
all_claims.source_passage: There are no significant shortcuts to intellectual competence.

all_claims.text: School-level educational research is less reliable and generalizable than logically necessary inferences drawn from basic scientific research.
all_claims.source_passage: school-based research into specific programs has proved to be less reliable and generalizable than logically necessary inferences from basic research.

all_claims.text: The foundational ideas of American schooling—natural development, individualism, and all-purpose skills—have been disconfirmed by multiple independent scientific perspectives.
all_claims.source_passage: The foundations of current American schooling—natural development, individualism, and all-purpose skills—have now been disconfirmed from multiple independent perspectives

all_claims.text: Developmental psychology has shown that 'natural development' is an unreliable guide for determining appropriate school content.
all_claims.source_passage: from developmental psychology, which shows that natural development is no reliable guide to appropriate school content

all_claims.text: Psycholinguistic research demonstrates that communication skills are dependent on domain-specific knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: from psycholinguistics, which shows that communication skills require domain-specific knowledge

all_claims.text: Only a well-rounded, knowledge-specific curriculum can impart necessary knowledge to all children and overcome inequality of opportunity.
all_claims.source_passage: Only a well-rounded, knowledge-specific curriculum can impart needed knowledge to all children and overcome inequality of opportunity.

all_claims.text: If a single large American school district adopts a content-specific curriculum districtwide, it will trigger a national shift in educational policy.
all_claims.source_passage: If one single big district does so, it will be a watershed event in our educational history. ... In time, with first-class results, other districts, even states, will adopt the districtwide policy by popular demand.

all_claims.text: Nation building and nation preserving was a conscious, explicit mission of 19th-century communal schools.
all_claims.source_passage: Nation building and nation preserving was a conscious mission of those communal schools.

all_claims.text: The dethroning of the post-romantic ideology of providential individualism is a necessary prerequisite for effective educational reform.
all_claims.source_passage: Once that outmoded and unsuccessful ideology is dethroned, effective educational reforms can take hold, but not before.

all_claims.text: Providing individual attention to some students in a school setting necessarily results in the neglect of others.
all_claims.source_passage: Both types of comment can be true, since individual attention to some students necessarily means individual neglect of others.

all_claims.text: The claim of child-centered education to accommodate the individual child holds powerful emotional appeal despite intellectual flaws.
all_claims.source_passage: Although no persuasive intellectual argument is left standing to excuse curricular incoherence, an emotional one still remains, and it is powerful.

all_claims.text: 19th-century textbook makers engaged in a 'benign conspiracy' to intentionally copy one another to foster patriotic sentiment.
all_claims.source_passage: Textbook makers contrived to copy one another in a benign conspiracy to foster a positive patriotic sentiment.

all_claims.text: Efforts to make communal knowledge more multicultural are admirable and have already successfully changed shared knowledge.
all_claims.source_passage: Those who would make that shared, taken-for-granted knowledge more multicultural are admirable... Their efforts have already changed our shared knowledge.

all_claims.text: The success of the American democratic experiment depends on preserving national unity as a community dedicated to common principles.
all_claims.source_passage: The success or failure of the experiment depended on preserving national unity as a far-flung community dedicated to common principles.

all_claims.text: The triple theme of American schools should be to 'think globally; act locally; contribute nationally.'
all_claims.source_passage: The triple theme of American schools should be “think globally; act locally; contribute nationally.”

all_claims.text: True 'living by ideas' requires the willingness to follow evidence to the opposite side of a question, even against one's own party and feelings.
all_claims.source_passage: That is what I call living by ideas: when one side of a question has long had your earnest support... still to be able to think, still to be irresistibly carried, if so it be, by the current of thought to the opposite side of the question.

all_claims.text: It is irrational to maintain skepticism about a scientific finding once intellectual triangulation from independent sources yields identical results.
all_claims.source_passage: Einstein proposed that if the computation of N should turn out the same from independent sources, then a rational person should cease to be a skeptic about the existence of atoms.

all_claims.text: Google Translate cannot effectively disambiguate language because it lacks access to explicitly given context.
all_claims.source_passage: That will not enable the program to disambiguate effectively since the context is not explicitly given.

all_claims.text: In knowledge-rich schools, teachers respond with increased enthusiasm and a deeper interest in their subject matters.
all_claims.source_passage: Teachers will respond with enthusiasm, as they always do in knowledge-rich schools, by enriching their own educations and becoming intensely interested in the subject matters.

all_claims.text: Ideas are reflected in architecture.
all_claims.source_passage: Ideas are reflected in architecture. Faith built cathedrals and mosques.

all_claims.text: Early twentieth-century school architecture symbolized the school's importance to the national and local community.
all_claims.source_passage: Their high flights of steps surmounted by high columns fairly shout: “The school is important to the local and national community.”

all_claims.text: Post-Second World War school architecture shifted to child-centered designs to emphasize the child’s individual world over communal significance.
all_claims.source_passage: The new architecture signaled that although the school was devoted to the larger community... the main emphasis would be on the children’s world, on encouraging their imaginations and developing their individual interests and personalities.

all_claims.text: The idea of a child’s natural development was a new and revolutionary theme in American education in the later nineteenth century.
all_claims.source_passage: It seems astonishing to us that the idea of the child’s natural development was new in American education when it became the central educational theme in the later nineteenth century.

all_claims.text: Eighteenth- and early nineteenth-century educational thinkers did not use the concepts of 'growth' and 'development' in their writings.
all_claims.source_passage: The words growth and development, so essential to educational thought today, are simply not found in their writings on the subject.

all_claims.text: Earlier educational thinking stressed correcting nature rather than following it.
all_claims.source_passage: Our earlier thinking about schooling stressed correcting nature rather than following her.

all_claims.text: Progressive education views sin as a result of social customs imposed on an innocent being rather than an inherent trait.
all_claims.source_passage: sin is felt to be a result of the imposition of human social customs upon the innocent being who comes straight from God.

all_claims.text: The classical aim of education was to correct nature through civilization, whereas the romantic aim is to correct civilization through nature.
all_claims.source_passage: The classical aim of education was to correct nature through civilization. The romantic aim of education is to correct civilization through nature.

all_claims.text: Horace Mann's reports show a distinct shift toward Romantic 'development' ideas by the 1840s.
all_claims.source_passage: By the 1840s, Horace Mann would refer to the “development” of the mind seventeen times in his reports to the Massachusetts Board of Education.

all_claims.text: Teaching principles like 'hands-on learning' are derived from the idea that natural methods are superior.
all_claims.source_passage: Out of the idea that natural is best have come the teaching principles that we immediately think of as key elements of progressive education—some of them, such as “hands-on learning,” appearing as early as Mann’s Seventh Annual Report

all_claims.text: By 1945, the natural development idea had become almost universal in American schools.
all_claims.source_passage: By 1945 the natural development idea had become almost universal in our schools, and has been so for the better part of a century

all_claims.text: Natural development theories are factually incorrect.
all_claims.source_passage: We now understand that these ideas are factually incorrect.

all_claims.text: Socialization is more natural to the human child than 'natural unfolding' is.
all_claims.source_passage: “Natural” unfolding is not as natural to the human child as socialization is.

all_claims.text: The dangerous social underside of the natural development idea is the unspoken assumption that poor children are less developed or less able.
all_claims.source_passage: The dangerous social underside of the “natural development” idea is the unspoken sense that poor children are less able and have slower development.

all_claims.text: The idea that education is a 'natural growth' or 'unfolding' is factually incorrect and morally wrong.
all_claims.source_passage: We now understand that these ideas are factually incorrect. 'Natural' unfolding is not as natural to the human child as socialization is. ... It is factually and morally wrong.

all_claims.text: Natural development as an educational principle intensifies social inequalities.
all_claims.source_passage: It intensifies inequalities.

all_claims.text: Education is inherently an induction into the adult tribe, a principle that held from the dawn of human groups until the Romantic period.
all_claims.source_passage: Education is inherently an induction into the adult tribe—and so it has been from the dawn of human social groups.

all_claims.text: The 'development' model of education demands from the child capacities they do not yet possess.
all_claims.source_passage: “Development” demands from the child what the child does not have.

all_claims.text: The American and French Revolutions together created a period of extreme historical optimism that fueled the idea of human progress through nature.
all_claims.source_passage: Coming after the American Revolution of 1776, the two revolutions taken together induced an orgy of historical optimism.

all_claims.text: The 'God-as-nature' idea (pantheism) became a widespread cultural sentiment only after the fall of the Bastille in 1789.
all_claims.source_passage: The God-as-nature idea was already in the air in Europe. But it did not become a widespread sentiment until after the fall of the Bastille.

all_claims.text: Post-revolutionary thinkers viewed Providence as manifest in the natural development of the individual from childhood to adulthood.
all_claims.source_passage: Providence was being made manifest in human history. God was immanent in us and in our affairs—and in our natural development from childhood to adulthood.

all_claims.text: Romanticism transformed the perception of the wilderness from a place of fear to a holy source of 'Nature'.
all_claims.source_passage: What used to be called 'wilderness' now became 'Nature.' Earlier the woods had been seen as fearsome places to be avoided... But now Lord Byron... could begin one of his most famous passages by declaiming: 'There is a pleasure in the pathless woods.'

all_claims.text: William Wordsworth was the Romantic poet who exerted the most significant influence on American educational theory, specifically on John Dewey.
all_claims.source_passage: But Wordsworth, not Byron, was the romantic poet who exercised the strongest influence in the United States. John Dewey praises his ability to penetrate into 'revelations of spirit, of meaning in nature'.

all_claims.text: Human growth requires external training within a specific culture, rather than being a purely internal unfolding.
all_claims.source_passage: The upshot: growth requires external training in humans, according to the culture they are educated into in every human group.

all_claims.text: Wordsworth's 'The Prelude' conceptualizes the growth of the human mind as a natural process guided by nature's divinity, analogous to a plant's growth.
all_claims.source_passage: The young boy and then the young poet grow like a plant under nature’s divine guidance toward 'years that bring the philosophic mind.'

all_claims.text: Hegel's 'The Phenomenology of Spirit' provided a secularized vehicle for the religious impulse of progress, creating a 'natural supernaturalism.'
all_claims.source_passage: Hegel fulfilled the imperative of making the religious impulse modern, acceptable, quasi-secular. He also gave substance and authority to the sentiment of progress.

all_claims.text: Hegel intentionally used secular and logical-sounding vocabulary to avoid censorship and charges of heresy from the clergy.
all_claims.source_passage: Hegel, of course, resisted the pantheistic p-word that so upset the clergy. As a university instructor seeking advancement, he could not afford to be censored or accused of heresy.

all_claims.text: English translations of Hegel, such as 'Phenomenology of Mind' and 'sublate,' intentionally obscured the religious and providential overtones of the original German terms.
all_claims.source_passage: English translations of his Phänomenologie des Geistes entitled it Phenomenology of Mind, ignoring the overtones of Heilige Geist—the Holy Spirit. ... Hegel’s British translators offered a misleadingly neutral technical term for aufheben, rendering it by the technical Latinate term sublate.

all_claims.text: The Hegelian concept of 'aufheben' (sublation) lacks a purely logical basis for its 'upward' or 'improving' trajectory without the underlying assumption of divine providence.
all_claims.source_passage: There’s no logical reason beyond divine providence why a purely logical process should go upward. Viruses could take over the world in a downward sweep.

all_claims.text: Hegel's influence on American thinkers like Dewey was consolidated by his ability to provide a world-picture that replaced organized religion while retaining its providential meaning.
all_claims.source_passage: Intellectuals were looking for a world picture to replace organized religion that would, like religion, give larger meaning to history and human experience. What they got in Hegel was a quasi-religion that gave a providential meaning to human history in acceptably secular garb.

all_claims.text: Friedrich Froebel’s Education of Man (1826) was the single most influential European book on early education in nineteenth-century America.
all_claims.source_passage: Froebel’s Education of Man (1826), itself the single most influential European book on early education in nineteenth-century America.

all_claims.text: Hegel and Froebel remained the primary intellectual touchstones for John Dewey’s mature exposition of progressive education in 1916.
all_claims.source_passage: As late as 1916, Hegel and Froebel still remained key touchstones in Dewey’s most ambitious and mature exposition of progressive education: Democracy and Education (1916).

all_claims.text: The term 'progressive education' originated in Switzerland with the 1828 book 'L’Éducation Progressive' by Albertine Necker de Saussure.
all_claims.source_passage: From Switzerland also came the first significant use of the term progressive education, the title of an 1828 book by Swiss writer Albertine Necker de Saussure.

all_claims.text: Pestalozzi was the Swiss educator primarily responsible for the child-centered and humane approach to teaching that influenced early American education.
all_claims.source_passage: Pestalozzi (1746–1827), who had been the early spokesperson for a more child-centered and humane approach to teaching.

all_claims.text: The chief intellectual influences on American progressive education were German pantheistic philosophers, specifically Hegel and Froebel.
all_claims.source_passage: The chief intellectual influences on American progressive education were German pantheistic philosophers: Hegel and Froebel.

all_claims.text: Hegel’s philosophy reassured educational thinkers that the processes of nature, history, and the mind are 'necessarily' progressive due to being infused by 'Absolute Spirit.'
all_claims.source_passage: The more profound sentiment, which Parker, Harris, Blow, and Dewey got from Hegel, was his confident reassurance that the processes of nature, of human history, and of the human mind are infused by “Absolute Spirit” and are thus “necessarily” progressive.

all_claims.text: The educational 'necessity' described by Hegel was accepted by his acolytes because they understood it to be referring to God.
all_claims.source_passage: Hegel imposed an odd use of the word necessity, which his acolytes accepted, no doubt because implicitly everyone knew he was ultimately talking about God.

all_claims.text: American educational history would have been better if progressive education had adopted Francis Parker's demanding view of the curriculum rather than his more contradictory ideas.
all_claims.source_passage: if progressive education had followed Parker’s demanding view of the curriculum, our educational history would have fared better.

all_claims.text: Francis Parker’s educational philosophy was grounded in a pantheistic view where a single central law controls both humanity and the universe.
all_claims.source_passage: Parker’s own pantheistic pronouncements were not dissimilar... 'One central law controls both man and the universe. The laws of the universe reveal the ego to itself.'

all_claims.text: Froebel's theory posits that any prescriptive or categorical education interferes with and destroys the divine unfolding of the child.
all_claims.source_passage: In view of the original soundness and wholeness of man, all arbitrary (active), prescriptive and categorical, interfering education in instruction and training must, of necessity, annihilate, hinder, and destroy.

all_claims.text: Parker combined a desire for a strong, common subject-matter curriculum with an emphasis on child-centered pedagogy.
all_claims.source_passage: While Parker wanted a strong, common subject-matter curriculum in the early grades, his emphasis was on pedagogy, on the active, visual, tactile (natural) experience of such a curriculum.

all_claims.text: W. T. Harris and Susan Blow attempted to reconcile naturalistic kindergarten methods with the academic mastery of subject matters.
all_claims.source_passage: W. T. Harris, who wanted to make sure that subject matters were studied and mastered, as did Harris’s fellow Hegelian Susan Blow, whose kindergarten was academic as well as naturalistic.

all_claims.text: John Dewey’s early educational philosophy was explicitly Hegelian and influenced by Romantic poets like Wordsworth, Keats, and Coleridge.
all_claims.source_passage: His first book, Psychology (1887), exhibits explicit Hegelian thinking. The book is a remarkable mixture of hard science and Hegel, with doses of Wordsworth, John Keats, and much borrowing from... Samuel Taylor Coleridge.

all_claims.text: Dewey used the distinction between 'fancy' and 'imagination' to differentiate between authentic child impulses and 'bad' ones.
all_claims.source_passage: He took from Coleridge the distinction between fancy and imagination. It was important to distinguish the two, because the good imaginations of the child had to be distinguished from the bad ones (fancy); only the good ones were sacred and authentic.

all_claims.text: Dewey viewed the child's cognitive development as the process by which the universal mind (deity) reproduces itself in the individual.
all_claims.source_passage: Dewey described the development of the child’s mind and imagination as an instance of the divine mind realizing itself in the human mind.

all_claims.text: The ultimate theological justification for child-centered education is the belief that child impulses are a manifestation of the divine will.
all_claims.source_passage: The child’s impulses are from God—indeed they are part of God’s own self-objectifying activity. To oppose those impulses is to oppose the will of God.

all_claims.text: Dewey’s concept of imagination was a direct recasting of Samuel Taylor Coleridge’s pantheistic definitions in Biographia Literaria.
all_claims.source_passage: That passage is a recasting by Dewey of a famous pantheistic passage in Coleridge’s Biographia Literaria (1817)

all_claims.text: The modern focus on 'imagination' in education represents a shift from Noah Webster's classical instructive model to a Romantic 'inner light' model.
all_claims.source_passage: It also represented a gradual shift from the instructive, molding conception of early education—the classical concept of Noah Webster—to the romantic, pantheistic idea of natural development.

all_claims.text: Modern educational practices retain the structure of pantheistic 'natural development' theory even though the underlying theology is no longer believed.
all_claims.source_passage: Few now believe in this pantheistic theology, but the husk of the inner light tradition has remained in our schooling, even without the explicit theological foundation.

all_claims.text: The educational distinction between 'imagination' and 'fancy' was a secular substitution for the theological distinction between 'God' and 'Satan.'
all_claims.source_passage: The new age substituted “imagination versus fancy” for ”God versus Satan.”

all_claims.text: Dewey’s faith in the inherent rightness of a child’s imagination was derived directly from European pantheism.
all_claims.source_passage: Dewey’s faith in the infallible rightness of the child’s imagination came directly from European pantheism.

all_claims.text: Early education shifted from Noah Webster's molding, instructive concept to a Romantic idea of natural development where imagination is viewed as a divine spark.
all_claims.source_passage: It also represented a gradual shift from the instructive, molding conception of early education—the classical concept of Noah Webster—to the romantic, pantheistic idea of natural development, in which encouraging the imagination of the child is in effect encouraging the divine spark, the inner light.

all_claims.text: The tradition of the 'inner light' persists in modern schooling as an unexamined cultural value, despite the loss of its explicit theological foundation.
all_claims.source_passage: Few now believe in this pantheistic theology, but the husk of the inner light tradition has remained in our schooling, even without the explicit theological foundation.

all_claims.text: Teaching children substantial knowledge of the world is more valuable to their future well-being than teaching 'imaginative' stories.
all_claims.source_passage: ”Traditionalists” maintained that teaching children substantial knowledge about the world is far more valuable to their future well-being than teaching fanciful, and sometimes not-very-good, stories whose only virtue is that they are “imaginative” and “encourage the �imagination.”

all_claims.text: Much of what is currently taught as imaginative literature in early grades is actually mindless 'fancy' rather than true imagination.
all_claims.source_passage: The shift from Coleridge on imagination to the current mindless approbation of what Coleridge would have called mere “fancy” marks the decadent empty husk of romanticism.

all_claims.text: Dewey’s concept of 'growth' is a secularized version of Hegel and Froebel’s 'development.'
all_claims.source_passage: Yet this change in vocabulary is just Froebel and Hegel in a new key. For if “growth” is a good thing, then it too must be just as progressive for Dewey as “development” was for Hegel and Froebel.

all_claims.text: Modern doctrines of 'readiness' and 'developmental appropriateness' are guiding ideas in teacher training that lack their original pantheistic authority.
all_claims.source_passage: The doctrines of individual “readiness” and “developmental appropriateness” are still guiding ideas in American teacher training, but the naturalistic approach is no longer clothed with the pantheistic authority of Hegel or Froebel.

all_claims.text: Modern cognitive science and developmental psychology do not actually support the constructivist or stage-theory interpretations of Jean Piaget used in schools.
all_claims.source_passage: Yet neither interpretation of Piaget is supported by current cognitive and developmental science—nor, as we have seen, by the later Piaget himself.

all_claims.text: The humane style of teaching introduced by the progressive movement is a valuable insight that should be preserved.
all_claims.source_passage: The progressive insistence on childhood as being a time of life with its own value... is an insight we must keep, along with its humane style of teaching.

all_claims.text: A vague, quasi-pantheistic Froebelian fundamentalism causes the ongoing resistance to systematic phonics and factual learning in early schooling.
all_claims.source_passage: A vague, quasi-pantheistic Froebelian fundamentalism accounts for our continued resistance to systematic phonics and “the piling up of facts” in early schooling.

all_claims.text: The deep-seated metaphysical beliefs supporting natural development are impervious to empirical evidence.
all_claims.source_passage: Such deep-seated metaphysical beliefs in support of natural development are impervious to evidence.

all_claims.text: The focus on individuality and a humane style of teaching inherited from progressive education has added to human happiness and should be preserved.
all_claims.source_passage: nality) has added to human happiness, and is an insight we must keep, along with its humane style of teaching.

all_claims.text: The aim of natural development in schools is still underpinned by a deep religious sentiment that children are innocent beings coming directly from God.
all_claims.source_passage: On the other hand, the deep religious sentiment underpinning the aim of natural development of the innocent young child who comes direct from God... is still powerful in the schools.

all_claims.text: Current educational sentiment views artificial impositions like 'rote learning' as inherently harmful and unnatural.
all_claims.source_passage: the sentiment that artificial impositions like “rote learning” are harmful and unnatural, is still powerful in the schools.

all_claims.text: New York City Schools Chancellor Carmen Fariña dismissed the superior results of the Core Knowledge program because of ideological certitude rather than data.
all_claims.source_passage: That explains the scornful dismissal by New York City Schools Chancellor Carmen Fariña of the superior results achieved by the Core Knowledge reading program in New York City. She had a deeper certitude than mere data could supply about what is best for children.

all_claims.text: Educational progress will continue to elude the United States until it overcomes 19th-century Middle European religious certitudes.
all_claims.source_passage: Until we overcome these historically contingent religious certitudes from Middle Europe of the early nineteenth century, progress will continue to elude us.

all_claims.text: Empathy with the child's point of view is compatible with imparting a strong common curriculum defined by the community.
all_claims.source_passage: Many superb teachers here and abroad have exemplified a pedagogy that exhibits empathy with childhood and the child’s point of view, yet imparts a strong common curriculum defined by the community.

all_claims.text: A strong common curriculum ensures both joy and competence for disadvantaged students.
all_claims.source_passage: A strong common curriculum is also compatible with ensuring joy and competence for disadvantaged students.

all_claims.text: The individualistic and natural-development aspects of progressive education are unsound and harmful to social justice.
all_claims.source_passage: We must reject what is unsound, and indeed deeply harmful to social justice and far too individualistic.

all_claims.text: Adopting sounder principles of socialization and fairness will reduce inequality and recover civic ideals.
all_claims.source_passage: In following sounder principles of socialization and fairness, we will reduce inequality and recover much-needed civic and communitarian ideals.

all_claims.text: In humans, cognitive and personal growth requires external training into a specific culture.
all_claims.source_passage: growth requires external training in humans, according to the culture they are educated into in every human group.

all_claims.text: The 18th-century rise of nonsectarian 'natural religion' was a direct cultural reaction to the exhaustion following 17th-century sectarian wars of religion.
all_claims.source_passage: Already in the mid eighteenth century, nonsectarian 'natural religion' was a big theme in Europe, with everyone heartily sick of the slaughter caused by the sectarian wars of religion in the seventeenth century.

all_claims.text: The architecture of cathedrals and mosques serves as historical evidence that human faith and abstract ideas are the primary drivers behind significant civic and religious structures.
all_claims.source_passage: Ideas are reflected in architecture. Faith built cathedrals and mosques.

all_claims.text: The Hegelian term 'aufheben' (sublate) contains an inherent providential meaning of 'lifting up' that is not logically required by a purely secular or logical process.
all_claims.source_passage: Similarly, Hegel’s verb for the logical progress of Geist through time is aufheben, a term that means 'to cancel' but keeps overtones of its positive root sense 'to lift up' (auf = 'up'; heben = 'lift'). There’s no logical reason beyond divine providence why a purely logical process should go upward.

all_claims.text: Understanding the contingent historical origins of educational ideas is necessary to undermine their current status as self-evident truths.
all_claims.source_passage: To know their contingent origins can help make them seem less self-evident, more vulnerable, a consummation devoutly to be wished.

all_claims.text: Hegel’s developmental theory represents a transformation of the scientific theory of evolution into an 'immanental teleology' where everything is grounded in a divine goal.
all_claims.source_passage: The scientific theory of the evolution of the universe was converted into an immanental teleology and a developmental theory. This reached its completion when he could postulate anything and everything as having its ultimate grounding in a Divine teleological goal.

all_claims.text: Marietta Johnson defined education not as a specific set of outcomes but as the state of a child being 'wholesomely, happily, intelligently employed'.
all_claims.source_passage: If the child is wholesomely, happily, intelligently employed, he is being educated!

all_claims.text: The recent educational focus on 'grit' and 'perseverance' constitutes a modern resurgence of the classical concept of 'training'.
all_claims.source_passage: 'Training' is making a comeback along with factual knowledge, perseverance, and 'grit.'

all_claims.text: Horace Mann’s support for multi-sensory instruction was explicitly justified by the theological premise that God provided five senses for effective learning.
all_claims.source_passage: God gave us five senses, Mann said; hence, education is most natural and effective if we use them all, and we do not limit ourselves to purely bookish, rote-learned activities.

all_claims.text: By the mid-19th century, the city of St. Louis had emerged as a major American center for Hegelian philosophy, comparable in influence to Harvard.
all_claims.source_passage: By the 1860s and 1870s St. Louis equaled Harvard as a center of Hegelian thought.

all_claims.text: The target population for the 1987 French study consisted of fifth-grade pupils enrolled in the public sector.
all_claims.source_passage: The target population in 1987 was that of fifth-grade pupils enrolled in France in the public sector.

all_claims.text: The percentage of primary school students in the French private sector remained stable at approximately 14 percent between 1987 and 2007.
all_claims.source_passage: It should be noted that the percentage of primary school students in the private sector remained stable over the twenty years (about 14 percent).

all_claims.text: In 2007, French students in the private sector achieved results equivalent to those in the public sector across literacy, numeracy, spelling, and grammar.
all_claims.source_passage: Moreover, in 2007, students in the private sector achieve results equivalent to those of the public sector, whether in literacy, numeracy, or in spelling and grammar.

all_claims.text: Centralizing and processing student responses via a web server allows for more reliable corrections and quality measurement than teacher-led scoring.
all_claims.source_passage: This system allowed for more reliable corrections to measure quality.

all_claims.text: The overall decline in mathematics performance in France between 1987 and 2007 was more than twice as large as the decline in reading.
all_claims.source_passage: The overall decline in math was more than twice the decline in reading: –.84 standard deviation in math, compared to –.37 standard deviation in reading.

all_claims.text: The decline in educational equity, measured by the spread between demographic groups, was four times greater in reading than in math in France.
all_claims.source_passage: The increase in spread between the top and bottom groups in math was .1 standard deviation, while the increase in spread in reading was four times as great: .4 standard deviation.

all_claims.text: Poor schooling has a significantly greater negative impact on equity in reading than it does on equity in math.
all_claims.source_passage: Poor schooling has a greater negative effect on equity in reading than in math. This finding is important for schooling as an agency of equal opportunity.

all_claims.text: Mathematics is primarily a school subject where instructional effectiveness tends to decline for all students uniformly.
all_claims.source_passage: Math is primarily a school subject. If the effectiveness of math instruction declines for some, it tends to decline for all.

all_claims.text: Reading performance is dependent upon general knowledge and vocabulary size, making it both a school and a home subject.
all_claims.source_passage: Reading is both a school and a home subject. It depends upon general knowledge and vocabulary size.

all_claims.text: Direct word study and skills-based curricula cannot achieve a significant increase in a student's vocabulary size.
all_claims.source_passage: But a significant increase in vocabulary size cannot be achieved through direct word study and a skills curriculum.

all_claims.text: A knowledge-based curriculum is far more effective at promoting equity than a skills-based curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: The knowledge curriculum can contribute far more to equity than the skills curriculum can. France has now demonstrated this forcefully.

all_claims.text: The adoption of whole-language methods led younger French teachers to reject the more effective phonics method for teaching decoding.
all_claims.source_passage: Indoctrinated in the superiority of whole-language methods, younger teachers spurned the far more effective method of phonics in teaching decoding skill.

all_claims.text: The rejection of systematic grammar and spelling exercises in favor of 'natural' approaches contributed to the decline in French student writing skills.
all_claims.source_passage: They also spurned exercises in grammar and spelling in favor of more “natural” approaches.

all_claims.text: The percentage of ten-year-old French students making more than twenty-five writing errors doubled between 1987 and 2007.
all_claims.source_passage: An analysis of the writing errors from ten-year-olds in the two periods showed that the percentage of students who made more than twenty-five errors doubled in the period, from 6 percent to 12 percent!

all_claims.text: The 1987 school selection, though limited to five jurisdictions, was statistically representative of the parent population.
all_claims.source_passage: In 1987, the selection of schools took place in just five jurisdictions, but the sample was found to be representative of the parent population.

all_claims.text: Decimal multiplication and division were removed from the French primary curriculum between 1987 and 2007.
all_claims.source_passage: multiplication and divisions of decimals were excluded as they are not in the present curriculum.

all_claims.text: Valid longitudinal comparison of student performance requires the identical repetition of test instructions across different years.
all_claims.source_passage: Instructions for the test were repeated identically in each year.

all_claims.text: To maintain assessment validity and prevent student fatigue, no more than one test sequence should be completed in a half-day session.
all_claims.source_passage: It was required not to complete more than one sequence in a half day.

all_claims.text: The variability in inter-rater agreement for reading items (dropping as low as 80% for some questions) has a negligible impact on final performance metrics.
all_claims.source_passage: The rate was more variable in reading: it dropped to 80 percent for some items, mostly due to hesitation between total and partial success, but had little impact on the final measure of performance.

all_claims.text: Schools must maximize their contribution to student knowledge and vocabulary in order to fulfill the social aim of equal opportunity.
all_claims.source_passage: The school contribution to knowledge and vocabulary is thus key for the aim of equal opportunity, and needs to be maximized.

all_claims.text: Between 1987 and 2007, France experienced a simultaneous decline in the effectiveness of instruction for both knowledge acquisition and decoding skills.
all_claims.source_passage: A confounding factor in this reckoning is that the effectiveness of instruction in both knowledge and in decoding declined in France.

all_claims.text: The increase in the performance spread between demographic groups was quantified as .1 standard deviation for math and .4 standard deviation for reading.
all_claims.source_passage: The increase in spread between the top and bottom groups in math was .1 standard deviation, while the increase in spread in reading was four times as great: .4 standard deviation.

all_claims.text: Between 1945 and 1952, American occupiers attempted to reform the Japanese school system by replacing 'moral training' with democratic training and introducing local control.
all_claims.source_passage: After World War II, when the Americans occupied Japan from 1945 to 1952, they intervened in the school system. They wanted Japanese children to learn democratic values. “Moral training” was abolished, to be replaced by training in democratic ideas. The Americans also introduced the local control idea, taking control of the schools and curriculum away from the central government and putting it into the hands of the municipalities.

all_claims.text: The Japanese public rejected American educational reforms and returned control to the central government in 1952.
all_claims.source_passage: Fortunately for Japan’s schools, the Americans left in 1952. The Japanese public did not support keeping the American reforms. They returned control of the schools to the central government

all_claims.text: Japan returned to a centralized system because decentralization failed and they believed in the value of moral training and a common curriculum.
all_claims.source_passage: They returned control of the schools to the central government, primarily because decentralization did not work very well, and secondly because they thought both moral training and a common curriculum were sound educational ideas.

all_claims.text: Japanese students consistently rank at or near the top of PISA rankings across all subjects.
all_claims.source_passage: In recent years Japanese students have ranked at or very near the top across all subjects in the PISA rankings.

all_claims.text: Japanese elementary schools effectively balance child-centered pedagogy with caring and humane environments.
all_claims.source_passage: I have long been an admirer of Japanese elementary schools, which are very child-centered in the good sense of the term... The schools are caring places, and are also very humane in pedagogy and content.

all_claims.text: Japanese students excel in science despite not beginning formal science study until grade 3.
all_claims.source_passage: Moreover, science study does not even begin until grade 3, and yet Japanese students score at the top of the rankings in science.

all_claims.text: The delay in Japanese science instruction may be a result of the high cognitive demands of the Japanese writing system.
all_claims.source_passage: The delay may be owing to the huge demands of the Japanese writing system.

all_claims.text: The Japanese science curriculum is a model of an intentionally and coherently structured curriculum designed to build rich content knowledge across grades.
all_claims.source_passage: I will reproduce a translation of Japan’s successful science curriculum in this appendix, because it seems to me a model of what is meant by a “curriculum intentionally and coherently structured to develop rich content knowledge within and across grades”

all_claims.text: Education should be understood as a process of socialization that includes aesthetic and evaluative elements.
